What It Means: Station reducing output power due to high internal temp — self-protecting by limiting charging speed.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system (fans, coolant, HEX). Check for obstructions.

What It Means: AC Board temp exceeded safe threshold. General overheating.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation, ambient temp, cooling components. Power cycle.

What It Means: ACB CPU overheating. Possible cooling failure or excessive load.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Port's AC Board section exceeded 95°C.
Recommended Action: Check head unit seating, blindmate connection. Replace power plate if persists.

What It Means: No power detected to a port. RCCB tripped or voltage too low.
Recommended Action: Confirm jumpers for single circuit. Check wiring. For dual circuit, check breaker. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB not operational. May be temporary during FW update.
Recommended Action: Wait 5 min if FW update in progress. Reboot. Contact engineering if doesn't recover.

What It Means: ACB firmware initialization failure.
Recommended Action: Check ACB menu in chassis-shell. Update SW via NOS. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Internal thread error in ACB firmware.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB firmware stack overflow — memory corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: ACB failed to initialize during startup.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware or replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: ADC error. Voltage/current readings may be unreliable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: CPU encountered fatal hardware or firmware issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Watchdog/DMA error — internal watchdog or memory access failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB self-test failed (CLKERR, FLASHERR, RAMERR, PWRERR, GFCIERR).
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Contact engineering. Replace ACB.

What It Means: GFCI detection circuit failed its diagnostic check.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: General internal power supply issue on ACB.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Flash memory error — firmware storage may be corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: RAM error — working memory failed or corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Internal timing circuit failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: ACB lost CAN bus communication with Falcon/UCB. Latched fault.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable (Pin 10 & 11). Verify impedance (120Ω). Replace Falcon/UCB. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Weld detection circuit not operating OR input voltage below 80V RMS. Does NOT mean relay is welded.
Recommended Action: Check input voltage. If below 200V, schedule site electrical check. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Power plate ID pins have illegal values.
Recommended Action: Check power plate alignment/seating. Verify blindmate. Replace power plate if persists.

What It Means: Relay that should be closed is open. Register bit: RSO.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if doesn't clear.

What It Means: Relay that should be open is closed. Register bit: RSC. Latched fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Test with another vehicle. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Circuit distribution error.
Recommended Action: Contact engineering.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Informational alert about power distribution.
Recommended Action: Monitor. No action unless accompanied by other faults.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Neutral wire error detected.
Recommended Action: Check neutral wiring. Contact engineering.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Voltage imbalance across phases.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring and phase balance.

What It Means: Power plate overheating at blindmate connection.
Recommended Action: Check head unit seating. Inspect blindmate for debris. Replace power plate if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: AC overcurrent — too much current in AC path.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace power plate/ACB if persists.

What It Means: DC leakage current to ground — safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: DC ground fault — direct current path to ground. Can trigger shunt trip (EU).
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: AC leakage current detected.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and RCCB.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: One or more AC input phases missing.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring. Verify all phases present.

What It Means: Non-recoverable ground fault. Occurs as relay closes (NA only). Auto-clears when EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for EV disconnect. If recurring, replace ACB.

What It Means: Relay coil GPIO in unexpected state. Register bit: RLYP.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if doesn't clear.

What It Means: Ground monitoring circuit itself has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Ground monitoring detected actual ground issue.
Recommended Action: Check station grounding. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Recoverable ground fault — may clear after EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for session end. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: General ground fault — current flowing to ground unexpectedly.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check wiring and grounding. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP6000 — EU only (NA has no RCCBs)
What It Means: Shunt trip activated. If right port faults — entire station turns off.
Recommended Action: Check RCCB. Rearm if tripped. Investigate root cause.

What It Means: Differential current sensing circuit issue. Register bit: GFERR.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. RMA head unit if doesn't resolve. Falcon can be kept.

What It Means: Same as ground-error. Register bit: GFERR.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. RMA head unit if doesn't resolve. Falcon can be kept.

What It Means: Station drawing more current than allowed.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: AC-specific overcurrent through charging circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and breaker. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Soft GFCI trip — minor ground fault.
Recommended Action: Usually self-clearing. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Hard GFCI trip — significant ground fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Ground monitoring detected fault.
Recommended Action: Check station grounding. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: GFCI self-test error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Relay stuck open. Can't energize charging cable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Stack overflow in ACC firmware.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Relay stuck closed. Safety issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: UART serial communication error.
Recommended Action: Check cable connections. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Hard ground fault detection circuit failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: GFCI sensing coil failed.
Recommended Action: Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Current sensing coil error.
Recommended Action: Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Voltage fluctuations on AC input.
Recommended Action: Check site power quality. Replace ACC if site power is OK.

What It Means: AC voltage out of acceptable range.
Recommended Action: Check incoming voltage. Verify breaker and wiring.

What It Means: J1772 pilot signal voltage incorrect.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit wiring. Test with another vehicle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Charging state machine timed out.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If recurring, escalate to engineering.

What It Means: ACC self-test didn't complete in time.
Recommended Action: Retry self-test. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: AC input frequency out of range.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Verify generator frequency if on backup power.

What It Means: Ground monitor error test failure. Often seen with zero energy dispense.
Recommended Action: Resend configurations. Reboot. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: CPU internal registers failed check.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: CPU instruction pointer corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: RAM error — working memory corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Flash memory error — firmware storage corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: CPU temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation. Usually self-clearing.

What It Means: Input cable temp elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Ensure proper torque on terminals.

What It Means: Output cable temp elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cable for damage.

What It Means: CPU exceeded critical temperature. Charging stops.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Input cable exceeded critical temp. Potential fire risk.
Recommended Action: Immediately check cable and connections. Inspect for loose terminals.

What It Means: Output cable exceeded critical temp.
Recommended Action: Check cable for damage. Check connector. Reduce charging current if possible.

What It Means: AC Power Meter lost CAN communication.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to ACPM. Power cycle. Replace ACPM if persists.

What It Means: ACPM firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS lost CAN communication with DCC.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Fan 1 RPM doesn't match expected speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan if persists.

What It Means: Board temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation and ambient temp.

What It Means: Board temp reached critical level.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Main 48V output below expected but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. May indicate degrading AuxPS.

What It Means: Main 48V output above expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if voltage stays high.

What It Means: External 48V rail below expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check load on external rail.

What It Means: External 48V rail above expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Main output drawing more current than expected.
Recommended Action: Check connected components. Monitor.

What It Means: External output overcurrent caused shutdown.
Recommended Action: Check what's connected to ext output. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Lost AC input power — main AC feed interrupted.
Recommended Action: Check breakers. Verify AC supply to station.

What It Means: Lost DC input — DC bus feeding AuxPS is down.
Recommended Action: Check DC connections. Power cycle.

What It Means: DC input low but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check power source.

What It Means: DC input above expected range.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check power source.

What It Means: AC input low — site may have voltage drop.
Recommended Action: Check AC supply voltage. Verify site wiring.

What It Means: AC input above expected — site voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Check AC supply. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if failed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 1 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if needed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 2 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if needed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 shorted — false high-temp readings.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 2 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 reading inconsistent.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if readings unreliable.

What It Means: NTC sensor 1 reading inconsistent with expected values.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if readings unreliable.

What It Means: NTC sensor 2 reading inconsistent with expected values.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if readings unreliable.

What It Means: Cooling Controller (CC) power channel has a short circuit. AuxPS shut down that rail.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CC wiring. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: External power channel has a short circuit.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check downstream devices. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: PBC/DCC power channel has a short circuit.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DCC/PBC wiring. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS internal temperature has reached critical fault level. Output may be shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system and ambient temp. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Cooling Controller channel drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check CC for shorts or excessive load. Power cycle. Replace CC or AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: External channel drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check what's connected to ext output. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: PBC/DCC channel drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check DCC/PBC for shorts. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Input voltage to AuxPS is above safe operating range. Could damage components.
Recommended Action: Check AC/DC input voltage. Verify site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: Input voltage to AuxPS is below safe operating range. Station may not function properly.
Recommended Action: Check AC/DC input voltage. Verify site wiring and breakers.

What It Means: Current imbalance detected between AuxPS output channels.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connected loads. Replace AuxPS if imbalance is significant.

What It Means: AuxPS temperature is above warning threshold but below critical fault.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation and ambient temp.

What It Means: Internal humidity has exceeded warning threshold. Moisture may be present inside station.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check door seals, cable glands. Inspect for water ingress.

What It Means: AuxPS fan tachometer error — RPM reading cannot be obtained or is invalid.
Recommended Action: Check fan connection. Replace AuxPS fan if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS fan has failed completely. No cooling airflow.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS fan assembly. Replace AuxPS if fan is integrated.

What It Means: Internal heater has failed. Station may not operate in cold environments.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS heater assembly or AuxPS module.

What It Means: Cooling Controller power rail is not good — voltage out of regulation.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CC connections. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: External power rail is not good — voltage out of regulation.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check ext connections. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: PBC/DCC power rail is not good — voltage out of regulation.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DCC connections. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS shut down the Cooling Controller power channel due to fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CC for shorts. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS shut down the external power channel.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check downstream devices. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS shut down the PBC/DCC power channel. Station controller has no power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DCC wiring. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS channel 1 overcurrent detected.
Recommended Action: Check connected load on ch1. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS channel 2 overcurrent detected.
Recommended Action: Check connected load on ch2. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS channel 3 overcurrent detected.
Recommended Action: Check connected load on ch3. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS lost CAN bus communication. Duplicate fault name for consistency across platforms.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Positive DC contact temperature above warning threshold. Cable connector is getting hot.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connector for debris. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Negative DC contact temperature above warning threshold.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connector for debris. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Positive DC contact exceeded critical temp — charging stopped for safety.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector pins. Check for damage/corrosion. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative DC contact exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector pins. Check for damage/corrosion. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg contacts exceeds warning threshold. One pin may have poor contact.
Recommended Action: Check connector alignment. Test with different vehicle.

What It Means: Positive terminal temperature above warning. Connection inside station may be loose.
Recommended Action: Check terminal torque inside contactor box.

What It Means: Negative terminal temperature above warning.
Recommended Action: Check terminal torque inside contactor box.

What It Means: Positive terminal reached fault-level temperature.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal connections. Re-torque. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Negative terminal reached fault-level temperature.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal connections. Re-torque. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg terminals exceeds threshold.
Recommended Action: Check both terminal connections. Re-torque.

What It Means: RTD temperature sensor 0 is open (disconnected). Reading unavailable.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace cable if sensor failed.

What It Means: RTD temperature sensor 1 is open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace cable if needed.

What It Means: RTD sensor 0 shorted — giving false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 2 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor. Replace cable if needed.

What It Means: RTD sensor 2 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 3 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor. Replace cable if needed.

What It Means: RTD sensor 3 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: Cable connector not fully inserted into vehicle inlet. Incomplete plug-in detected.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector. Check connector latch.

What It Means: Positive terminal overtemp warning — elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check terminal connections.

What It Means: Negative terminal overtemp warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check terminal connections.

What It Means: Positive terminal exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal. Re-torque. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative terminal exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal. Re-torque. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive cable body temp elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Reduce current if possible. Check cooling.

What It Means: Negative cable body temp elevated.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Reduce current if possible.

What It Means: Positive cable body exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for damage. Check cooling. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative cable body exceeded critical temp.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for damage. Check cooling. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: RTD temperature data is stale — sensor not reporting fresh readings. Safety concern.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensor connections. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: CAN communication with cable controller is intermittent.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Monitor.

What It Means: CAN data from cable is stale — cable controller not sending updates.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Cable temperature data is invalid — readings are out of expected range.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensor connections. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Standard 200A cable is too hot — approaching thermal limit at 200A charging rate.
Recommended Action: Reduce current. Check cable and connector. Check cooling system.

What It Means: Contact temperature difference exceeds 4°C. One pin may have poor contact.
Recommended Action: Check connector. Test with different vehicle.

What It Means: General RTD sensor fault. One or more cable temp sensors have failed.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensors. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Cable temperature has reached alert level — charging stopped. Legacy fault code 905.
Recommended Action: Check cable for damage. Try different vehicle. Escalate to #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Cable temperature near max threshold — charging speed reduced. Legacy fault code 904.
Recommended Action: Certain vehicles cause connector heating. Try different vehicle. Escalate if persists on multiple vehicles.

What It Means: Proximity pilot (PP) signal error — cable not properly detected or proximity circuit fault.
Recommended Action: Check connector seating. Check PP circuit wiring. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Cable insulation resistance test failed — potential safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for physical damage. Megger test cable. Replace cable if failed.

What It Means: Cable temperature alert — exceeded max safe temp. Charging stopped immediately.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable and connector. Check cooling. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Cable temperature warning — approaching max threshold. Charging speed may be reduced (de-rated).
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connector contact. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Cable temperature caused station to reduce output power. Self-protecting by limiting current.
Recommended Action: Check cable condition. Check cooling system. Usually self-clears when temp drops.

What It Means: Cable cut detected. Physical disconnection or break in charging cable. Lockout fault — station blocked until cleared by technician.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Check for vandalism. Station needs replacement or cable repair. Clear lockout via installer app or console.

What It Means: Cable controller lost communication with station controller. CAN or Ethernet link failure.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling to cable controller. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Cable controller firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: PLC (Power Line Communication) vehicle comms lost. Station cannot communicate with vehicle over CCS.
Recommended Action: Check PLC board. Retry session. Try different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: PLC hardware or firmware error. CCS communication circuit failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Cable configuration detected is invalid — EEPROM data doesn't match expected cable type.
Recommended Action: Check cable EEPROM. Verify correct cable installed. Replace cable if configuration is wrong.

What It Means: Cable ampacity configuration is invalid — the max current rating stored doesn't match the physical cable.
Recommended Action: Verify cable rating. Check EEPROM data. Replace cable or reconfigure.

What It Means: Cable controller board exceeded critical temp — shut down for safety.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Check ambient temp. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Cable cut detected by the cable controller. Physical cable damage or disconnection. Lockout fault.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for vandalism/damage. Replace cable. Clear lockout via installer app or console.

What It Means: Cable controller's internal power supply has failed. Board cannot operate.
Recommended Action: Replace cable controller.

What It Means: EEPROM on cable controller has failed. Cable identity and configuration data lost.
Recommended Action: Replace cable controller or cable.

What It Means: Safety CPU register check failed. The safety processor's registers are corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety non-stack register check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety floating-point register 1 check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety floating-point register 2 check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety PRIMASK register check failed — interrupt masking is corrupt.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety special register check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety program counter register failed — firmware execution integrity compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety stack check failed — stack corruption detected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety RAM check failed — memory corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety NVM (non-volatile memory) check failed — stored data corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety clock check failed — timing integrity compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety MPU (Memory Protection Unit) fault — unauthorized memory access detected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Safety strap configuration is invalid — hardware config pins are wrong.
Recommended Action: Check cable controller hardware. Replace cable controller.

What It Means: Ethernet communication with cable controller lost. Temporary network disruption.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet cable. Power cycle. Usually self-recovers.

What It Means: Ethernet communication with cable controller has permanently failed.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet cable and switch. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: EV diode check failed — the vehicle's diode appears shorted. Station detects abnormal pilot voltage.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If fault persists with all vehicles, replace cable controller.

What It Means: Card reader is not operational. Reader not responding to commands or failed to initialize. RFID/NFC/credit card taps will not work.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Check card reader cable seating. Restart card reader service. Replace CPNK/card reader if persists. CP6000 is not responding to RFID card taps

What It Means: Card reader firmware upgrade failed. Reader may still function on old firmware.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate to engineering if persists.

What It Means: Apple VAS (Value Added Services) key is not present on the card reader. Apple Wallet tap-to-charge won't work.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure card reader via NOS. Update firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: DUKPT encryption configuration failed on card reader. Payment processing may not work. Often seen after OCPP migration.
Recommended Action: Restart card reader service. Power cycle. Check PaymentGateway connectivity. Escalate if persists — may need manual DUKPT reconfiguration. PQE-16392: SN: 231014010304 - HALTON HONDA / FAST CHARGER (Case# 15684844) - Burlington, Ontario - (RFID card reader issue) - CPE250C-500-CCS1-CHDPending HW Update

What It Means: Apple VAS not supported on this card reader firmware or hardware. NXP library fell back to ChargePoint library. Apple Wallet won't work.
Recommended Action: Update card reader firmware. Check for SPI/interrupt issues. Station still charges via RFID/credit card. STNSVC-7605: Kestrel-Fleet-NPI: Fault code "urn:fault:cardreader:applevas-not-supported" in the "Diagnostics" tab does not show the "Fault Description"

What It Means: UIC card reader firmware does not support the required encryption standard. Payment card processing may fail or be disabled.
Recommended Action: Update card reader firmware to a version that supports current encryption. Escalate if firmware is already latest.

What It Means: Left fan bank has been disabled. Cooling capacity is reduced. Station may derate.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions/damage. Power cycle. Replace fan if persists.

What It Means: Right fan bank has been disabled. Cooling capacity reduced.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions/damage. Power cycle. Replace fan if persists.

What It Means: Bucket/lower fan tray has been disabled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray connection. Power cycle. Replace fan tray if persists.

What It Means: Left fan motor is locked — rotor is jammed and cannot spin. Fan drawing high current but not moving.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstruction (debris, ice). Replace fan assembly if motor seized.

What It Means: Right fan motor is locked/jammed.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstruction. Replace fan assembly if motor seized.

What It Means: Coolant pump has failed completely. No coolant circulation — station will overheat and derate or shut down.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Power cycle. Replace coolant pump. Check for coolant leaks.

What It Means: Air trapped in the coolant pump. Pump is running but not circulating coolant effectively.
Recommended Action: Bleed air from coolant system. Check coolant level. Top up if low. Power cycle pump.

What It Means: Coolant level is below minimum. Risk of overheating and pump damage from dry running.
Recommended Action: Top up coolant. Check for leaks in hoses, fittings, and HEX. Replace pump if damaged by dry run.

What It Means: Left fan drawing more current than expected — motor may be failing or obstructed.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan if motor is degrading.

What It Means: Right fan drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan if motor is degrading.

What It Means: Bucket fan tray drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan tray if persists.

What It Means: Left fan has open circuit — wiring disconnected or motor coil broken. Fan is not powered.
Recommended Action: Check fan wiring and connectors. Replace fan if coil is broken.

What It Means: Right fan has open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check fan wiring. Replace fan if needed.

What It Means: Bucket fan tray has open circuit — not receiving power.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan tray if needed.

What It Means: Coolant pump drawing overcurrent — motor may be failing, impeller jammed, or fluid blockage.
Recommended Action: Check for blockage in coolant lines. Check pump impeller. Replace pump if persists.

What It Means: Coolant pump has open circuit — wiring disconnected or motor failed. Pump is not powered.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Replace pump if motor is dead.

What It Means: CCB lost CAN bus communication with station controller. Cooling system status unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to CCB. Power cycle. Replace CCB if persists.

What It Means: Left fan bank running but no RPM feedback — tachometer signal not received. Fan may be spinning but can't be verified, or fan is stalled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tachometer wiring. Replace fan if tach signal is lost.

What It Means: Right fan bank no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check fan tachometer wiring. Replace fan if needed.

What It Means: Bucket fan tray no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray tachometer. Replace fan tray if persists.

What It Means: Coolant pump running but no RPM feedback. Can't verify pump is actually circulating.
Recommended Action: Check pump tachometer wiring. Replace pump if RPM signal is lost.

What It Means: All fan banks disabled — no active cooling. Station will overheat rapidly and shut down.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention required. Check all fans. Power cycle. Replace CCB or fans as needed.

What It Means: CCB board temperature above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation and ambient temp.

What It Means: CCB board temperature reached critical level — may shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Power cycle. Replace CCB if persists.

What It Means: Coolant level sensor is disconnected. Station can't monitor coolant level — safety risk.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring. Reconnect sensor. Replace sensor if failed.

What It Means: Same as above — sensor disconnected, coolant level unknown.
Recommended Action: Check and reconnect sensor.

What It Means: Fan tray (generic) drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan tray if motor degrading.

What It Means: Fan tray 1 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan tray 1.

What It Means: Fan tray 2 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan tray 2.

What It Means: Fan tray 3 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan tray 3.

What It Means: Fan tray (generic) has open circuit — not powered.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan tray.

What It Means: Fan tray 1 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan tray 1.

What It Means: Fan tray 2 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan tray 2.

What It Means: Fan tray 3 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan tray 3.

What It Means: Fan (generic) running but no RPM feedback — can't verify fan is spinning.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer wiring. Replace fan if signal lost.

What It Means: Fan tray 1, fan 1 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Fan tray 1, fan 2 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Fan tray 2, fan 1 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Fan tray 2, fan 2 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Fan tray 3, fan 1 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Fan tray 3, fan 2 — no RPM feedback.
Recommended Action: Check tachometer. Replace fan.

What It Means: Coolant pump overcurrent — motor may be failing or impeller jammed.
Recommended Action: Check for blockage. Replace pump if persists.

What It Means: Coolant pump open circuit — not powered.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Replace pump.

What It Means: Pump detected dry run — running without sufficient coolant. Risk of pump damage and overheating.
Recommended Action: Stop station immediately. Check coolant level. Top up. Check for leaks. Replace pump if damaged.

What It Means: Pump rotor is locked — motor jammed. No coolant flow.
Recommended Action: Check for debris in pump. Replace pump.

What It Means: Pump failed to start — motor didn't spin up during startup sequence.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Power cycle. Replace pump if persists.

What It Means: Pump's internal overcurrent protection triggered.
Recommended Action: Check for blockage or impeller issue. Replace pump.

What It Means: Pump running but no RPM signal — can't confirm coolant is circulating.
Recommended Action: Check pump tachometer. Replace pump if signal lost.

What It Means: Coolant level sensor disconnected — level unknown. Safety risk.
Recommended Action: Reconnect sensor. Replace sensor if failed.

What It Means: Coolant level critically low. Risk of overheating, pump damage, and station shutdown.
Recommended Action: Top up coolant immediately. Check for leaks. Replace pump if damaged by dry run.

What It Means: Coolant level below warning threshold but not yet critical.
Recommended Action: Schedule coolant top-up. Check for slow leaks.

What It Means: All fan trays disabled — no active air cooling. Station will overheat.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check all fans. Power cycle. Replace cooling controller or fans.

What It Means: Multiple RTD temperature sensors have failed simultaneously. Temperature monitoring severely degraded.
Recommended Action: Replace cooling controller.

What It Means: Generic RTD sensor disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: RTD sensor at HEX (Heat Exchanger) inlet is disconnected. Can't monitor incoming coolant temp.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring at HEX inlet.

What It Means: RTD sensor at HEX outlet disconnected. Can't monitor outgoing coolant temp.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring at HEX outlet.

What It Means: Ambient temperature RTD sensor disconnected. Station can't factor ambient temp into cooling decisions.
Recommended Action: Check ambient sensor wiring.

What It Means: Generic RTD sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or cooling controller.

What It Means: HEX inlet RTD sensor shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or cooling controller.

What It Means: HEX outlet RTD sensor shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or cooling controller.

What It Means: Ambient RTD sensor shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or cooling controller.

What It Means: HEX inlet coolant temp above warning — coolant coming into HEX is warmer than expected.
Recommended Action: Check fan operation. Check for airflow obstructions.

What It Means: HEX outlet coolant temp above warning — HEX not cooling effectively.
Recommended Action: Check fans, airflow, HEX for blockage or fouling.

What It Means: Ambient temperature above warning. Station in hot environment.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Station may derate. No hardware fix — environmental.

What It Means: HEX inlet temp reached critical — coolant is dangerously hot.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system immediately. Fans, pump, coolant level, airflow.

What It Means: HEX outlet temp critical — HEX is not removing enough heat.
Recommended Action: Check HEX for fouling/blockage. Check fans. Clean or replace HEX.

What It Means: Ambient temp reached fault level — extreme heat environment. Station will derate or shut down.
Recommended Action: Environmental condition. Station may need shade structure or relocated.

What It Means: 12V supply to cooling controller has failed. Cooling system is not powered.
Recommended Action: Check 12V supply wiring. Check AuxPS 12V rail. Replace cooling controller if persists.

What It Means: Generic fan tray disabled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Fan tray 1 disabled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray 1. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Fan tray 2 disabled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray 2. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Fan tray 3 disabled.
Recommended Action: Check fan tray 3. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Coolant pump disabled — no coolant circulation. Station will overheat.
Recommended Action: Check pump. Power cycle. Replace pump or cooling controller.

What It Means: Cooling controller board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Cooling controller board temp critical — may shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace cooling controller if persists.

What It Means: Cooling controller lost CAN communication with station controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace cooling controller if persists.

What It Means: Power Limited (PL) station is derating output because coolant pump has a fault. Charging continues at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Fix pump fault first. Check pump wiring, coolant level. Replace pump if needed.

What It Means: Left fan bank has failed or is not spinning at expected RPM.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan. Mapped to ccb:fan-fault-left-bank-no-rpm-feedback in unified code.

What It Means: Right fan bank has failed or is not spinning.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Replace fan. Mapped to ccb:fan-fault-right-bank-no-rpm-feedback.

What It Means: Coolant pump has failed. No coolant circulation.
Recommended Action: Replace pump. Check for leaks. Mapped to ccb:coolant-pump-failed.

What It Means: Air lock in coolant pump. Pump spinning but not moving fluid.
Recommended Action: Bleed air from system. Top up coolant. Mapped to ccb:coolant-pump-air-lock.

What It Means: Fan tray is missing from the module carrier slot. Fan not physically present.
Recommended Action: Reseat fan tray. Verify hardware installation.

What It Means: Coolant level critically low.
Recommended Action: Top up coolant. Check for leaks. Mapped to ccb:coolant-low-level.

What It Means: Three-phase gate driver has faulted. Power module can't switch properly. Charging stops.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Three-phase gate driver warning — approaching fault threshold but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. May escalate to full fault. Schedule maintenance.

What It Means: Coolant at medium-low level — not critical yet but trending down.
Recommended Action: Schedule coolant top-up. Check for slow leaks.

What It Means: Fan fault on the power module carrier. Cooling for power modules is compromised.
Recommended Action: Check power module carrier fans. Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Airflow is constricted at a low level — partial blockage. Station may derate under load.
Recommended Action: Check air filters and vents. Remove obstructions.

What It Means: Airflow is severely constricted — significant blockage. Station will derate or overheat.
Recommended Action: Immediately clear obstructions. Check filters, vents, and fan operation.

What It Means: Positive DC contact on liquid-cooled cable is above warning temp. Cooling may not be keeping up.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow to cable. Check connector for debris.

What It Means: Negative DC contact on liquid-cooled cable above warning temp.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow. Check connector.

What It Means: Positive contact exceeded critical temp — charging stopped. Liquid cooling failed to keep contact cool.
Recommended Action: Check coolant flow to cable. Check for kinks in coolant hose. Inspect connector pins. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative contact exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check coolant flow. Check hoses. Inspect connector. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: CAN communication with cooled cable controller is intermittent.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Monitor.

What It Means: CAN data from cooled cable is stale — cable controller not sending updates.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Cooled cable temperature data is invalid — readings out of range.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensor connections. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive terminal inside station (contactor box) reached fault temp. Connection may be loose.
Recommended Action: Re-torque terminal. Inspect for damage. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative terminal reached fault temp.
Recommended Action: Re-torque terminal. Inspect for damage. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive terminal exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal. Re-torque. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Negative terminal exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect terminal. Re-torque. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Standard 200A liquid-cooled cable is too hot — approaching thermal limit at 200A charging rate. Cooling system not keeping up.
Recommended Action: Reduce current. Check coolant flow to cable. Check cooling system (pump, HEX, fans).

What It Means: Positive terminal temp inside station above warning. Connection may be loosening.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check terminal torque inside contactor box.

What It Means: Negative terminal temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check terminal torque.

What It Means: Positive terminal overtemp warning — elevated but not yet at shutdown threshold.
Recommended Action: Check terminal connections. Re-torque. Schedule maintenance.

What It Means: Negative terminal overtemp warning.
Recommended Action: Check terminal connections. Re-torque.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg connector contacts exceeds threshold. One pin may have poor contact with vehicle inlet.
Recommended Action: Check connector alignment. Clean connector pins. Test with different vehicle.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg terminals (inside station) exceeds threshold. One terminal may be loose.
Recommended Action: Re-torque both terminals. Inspect for damage.

What It Means: Positive cable body temp has reached fault level. Cable insulation may be degrading.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for physical damage. Check coolant flow through cable. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative cable body temp at fault level.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Check coolant flow. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive cable body exceeded critical temp — charging stopped immediately. Coolant system failure or cable damage.
Recommended Action: Check coolant pump, hoses for kinks. Inspect cable for damage. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative cable body exceeded critical temp — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check coolant system. Inspect cable. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive cable body temp above warning — trending warm.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Negative cable body temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Positive cable body overtemp warning — approaching shutdown threshold.
Recommended Action: Check coolant flow. Reduce current if possible.

What It Means: Negative cable body overtemp warning.
Recommended Action: Check coolant flow. Reduce current if possible.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg cable bodies exceeds threshold. One conductor may have higher resistance or less cooling.
Recommended Action: Check coolant distribution in cable. Inspect cable for damage.

What It Means: Connector housing temperature above warning. The plastic/metal housing around the connector is warm.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check for direct sun exposure. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Connector housing temp reached fault level. Housing overheating — potential safety concern.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector housing. Check coolant flow. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Proximity Pilot (PP) signal is abnormal. The signal that tells the station about cable current rating is out of range.
Recommended Action: Check PP resistor in connector. Check wiring. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Shock/impact sensor on cable detected minor impact. Cable may have been dropped or hit.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cable and connector for physical damage.

What It Means: Shock sensor error — sensor circuit has a problem. Can't reliably detect impacts.
Recommended Action: Check shock sensor wiring. Replace cable or sensor module if persists.

What It Means: Shock sensor detected major impact — potential cable/connector damage. Lockout may be triggered.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable and connector thoroughly for damage (cracks, bent pins, coolant leak). Replace cable if damaged. Clear lockout via installer app.

What It Means: Coolant leakage sensor detected minor moisture. Small leak may be developing.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Inspect cable fittings and hose connections for moisture.

What It Means: Leakage sensor circuit error — sensor itself may have failed. Can't reliably detect leaks.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring. Replace cable or sensor module if persists.

What It Means: Coolant leakage detected — significant coolant leak from cable. Safety hazard. Station should be shut down.
Recommended Action: Stop station immediately. Inspect entire cable for coolant leak. Check fittings, hose connections, connector housing. Replace cable.

What It Means: General RTD sensor fault on cooled cable. One or more temp sensors have failed.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensors. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Terminal temperature difference exceeds 4°C. One terminal may have higher resistance.
Recommended Action: Re-torque both terminals. Inspect for corrosion.

What It Means: RTD sensor 0 open circuit — disconnected. Fault-level alert (more severe than simple open).
Recommended Action: Check sensor. Replace cable if sensor failed.

What It Means: RTD sensor 0 shorted — giving false readings. Fault-level alert.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 0 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: RTD sensor 0 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 1 open — fault-level.
Recommended Action: Check sensor. Replace cable if needed.

What It Means: RTD sensor 1 shorted — fault-level.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 1 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: RTD sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 2 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: RTD sensor 2 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD sensor 3 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: RTD sensor 3 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: RTD data is stale — sensors not reporting fresh readings. Safety concern — temp monitoring is blind.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensor connections. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: RFID reader firmware update failed. Reader may still function on old firmware.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Data partition on the station controller is full. Station may stop logging, storing sessions, or accepting updates.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Delete old data. Reboot. Escalate to engineering if persists. Maps to cpnk:data-partition-full in unified code.

What It Means: Station has exceeded the maximum number of grace/offline sessions. No more sessions allowed until station reconnects to cloud.
Recommended Action: Restore network connectivity. Station needs to sync with cloud to clear.

What It Means: The EVSE (charging controller) is unreachable from the station controller. Internal communication failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check internal cabling. Replace DCC or Falcon if persists.

What It Means: A thermal event has occurred — something inside the station got dangerously hot. Charging stopped for safety.
Recommended Action: Check all temperature sensors. Inspect cooling system. Check for burned components. Escalate if needed.

What It Means: Gesture sensor IC firmware update failed. Gesture-based UI interactions may not work.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Non-critical — station still charges.

What It Means: Input power meter has failed. Station can't accurately measure input power. Billing accuracy may be affected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: Gesture sensor has failed. Touchless UI gestures won't work. Station still charges normally.
Recommended Action: Replace CPNK/gesture sensor if needed. Non-critical.

What It Means: RFID reader has completely failed. No RFID card tap authentication possible.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check card reader cable. Replace CPNK/card reader if persists.

What It Means: Secondary display has failed. The smaller/secondary screen is not working.
Recommended Action: Check display cable. Replace display. Non-critical — primary display still works.

What It Means: Speaker communication has failed. Audio feedback (beeps, voice prompts) won't work.
Recommended Action: Check speaker cable. Replace speaker. Non-critical. Maps to cpnk:speaker-comms-failed.

What It Means: Ultrasonic sensor (proximity detection) communication failed. Auto-wake or presence detection won't work.
Recommended Action: Check sensor cable. Replace sensor. Non-critical — station still charges.

What It Means: Data partition on CPNK is full. Station may stop storing session data, logs, or accepting firmware updates.
Recommended Action: Clear logs and old data. Reboot. May need manual cleanup via shell. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Temporary partition on CPNK is full. Firmware updates and temporary file operations will fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files. If persists, manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: Speaker communication failed. Audio feedback won't work. Station still charges.
Recommended Action: Check speaker cable. Replace speaker module. Non-critical.

What It Means: Screen brightness is stuck at maximum and won't dim. Backlight control circuit issue.
Recommended Action: Reboot. Update firmware. Replace display board if persists. Non-critical — station still charges.

What It Means: Coolant System Controller lost communication with station controller. Coolant quality monitoring is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace CSCTRL if persists.

What It Means: CSCTRL firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Coolant electrical conductivity is above warning threshold. Coolant is becoming conductive — impurities are building up. Coolant may need replacement soon.
Recommended Action: Schedule coolant replacement. Monitor conductivity trend.

What It Means: Coolant conductivity has reached fault level — coolant is too conductive. Risk of electrical arcing or leakage through coolant. Safety concern.
Recommended Action: Replace coolant immediately. Flush system. Check for contamination source.

What It Means: A DC power module has faulted. Module stopped converting AC to DC. Station may continue on remaining modules at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If persists, identify which module faulted (via DCC logs). Replace power module.

What It Means: Cable insulation check detected a short circuit. DC cables or connector may be damaged. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable and connector for damage. Megger test cable. Replace cable if shorted.

What It Means: Station detected an invalid or unsupported cable. EEPROM data doesn't match expected configuration.
Recommended Action: Verify correct cable is installed. Check cable EEPROM. Replace cable if configuration is wrong.

What It Means: The paired station (in a power-share configuration) has a critical fault. This station may be affected if it depends on shared power.
Recommended Action: Check paired station for faults. Fix paired station first.

What It Means: Critical internal communication failure. Station controller cannot talk to one or more critical subsystems.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all internal cables. Replace Falcon/DCC if persists.

What It Means: The vehicle ended the charging session (not the station). This is informational — the EV's BMS decided to stop charging.
Recommended Action: No action needed. Normal behavior. Vehicle battery may be full or BMS decided to stop.

What It Means: Vehicle sent an invalid communication sequence during CCS/CHAdeMO handshake. Protocol violation by vehicle.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If happens with all vehicles, check PLC board and cable controller.

What It Means: Vehicle reported a voltage that is outside the station's supported range. Station can't match the vehicle's voltage request.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module voltage range.

What It Means: Vehicle failed to shut down properly at end of session. Current didn't ramp to zero as expected. Contactors opened under load.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists, check contactors for damage. Replace contactors if arc damage found.

What It Means: Power connection between paired stations has an error. Power sharing is not working.
Recommended Action: Check power cables between paired stations. Verify configuration.

What It Means: Communication with paired station lost. Power sharing suspended.
Recommended Action: Check communication cable between stations. Power cycle both stations.

What It Means: Paired station configuration mismatch. The two stations don't agree on power sharing settings.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure paired station settings. Ensure matching firmware versions.

What It Means: Multiple concurrent sessions detected on a single port — should not happen. Software or hardware glitch.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Cable was unplugged during session. Informational — normal if driver removed cable.
Recommended Action: No action if intentional. If unexpected, check connector latch mechanism.

What It Means: Session power management failed. Station couldn't properly manage power delivery during the session.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules. Escalate if recurring.

What It Means: GPIO (General Purpose Input/Output) failure on DCC. Hardware control pins not responding.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Cable I/O failure. The station can't read or control cable signals (pilot, proximity, lock).
Recommended Action: Check cable controller wiring. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: +12V internal supply is out of range. Too high or too low.
Recommended Action: Check DCC power supply. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: -12V internal supply is out of range.
Recommended Action: Check DCC power supply. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: CCS-A (Combo 1) control pilot voltage is out of range. Station can't properly communicate with the vehicle via pilot signal.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: CCS-B (Combo 2) control pilot voltage is out of range.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: CCS-A proximity pilot signal out of range. Station can't detect cable connection properly.
Recommended Action: Check proximity circuit and connector. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: CCS-B proximity pilot signal out of range.
Recommended Action: Check proximity circuit. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: DCC temperature sensor has completely failed. Station can't monitor DCC temperature.
Recommended Action: Replace DCC or temp sensor.

What It Means: DCC temperature sensor reading is erratic but sensor is still partially functional.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace DCC if readings become unreliable.

What It Means: EEPROM read error on DCC. Configuration or calibration data couldn't be read.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If persists, replace DCC. EEPROM may be corrupted.

What It Means: PLC board 0 (port 1 CCS communication) is not responding. Vehicle communication impossible on this port.
Recommended Action: Check PLC board seating. Power cycle. Replace PLC board if persists.

What It Means: PLC board 1 (port 2 CCS communication) is not responding.
Recommended Action: Check PLC board seating. Power cycle. Replace PLC board if persists.

What It Means: DC current sensor is not responding. Station can't measure charging current — safety system is blind.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check current sensor wiring. Replace current sensor or DCC if persists.

What It Means: Isolation Monitor CAN communication failed. Station can't verify DC cable/system isolation — safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CAN cable to IsoMon. Replace IsoMon module if persists.

What It Means: Relay 1 (main DC contactor) failed the open check — relay should be open but voltage detected across it. Relay may be welded shut.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If persists, replace contactor 1. Check for arc damage.

What It Means: Relay 1 failed the close check — relay should be closed but no current path detected. Relay may be stuck open or coil failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 1 if persists.

What It Means: Relay 1 not-closed check failed — auxiliary contacts don't confirm relay position. Contactor feedback mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check auxiliary contact wiring. Replace contactor 1 if persists.

What It Means: Relay 2 failed open check — may be welded shut.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Relay 2 failed close check — stuck open or coil failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Relay 2 auxiliary contact feedback mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Relay 3 (precharge contactor) failed open check — may be welded.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Relay 3 failed close check — stuck open or coil failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Relay 3 auxiliary contact feedback mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: All relays failed the open check simultaneously. Multiple contactors may be welded or control circuit has failed. Severe safety issue.
Recommended Action: Do not attempt to charge. Power cycle. Inspect all contactors for welding/arc damage. Replace contactors and DCC if needed. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: System voltage check failed during self-test. Internal voltage rails are out of specification.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DCC power supply. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: RISO (insulation resistance) state check failed. Station can't verify isolation between DC output and ground. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon module. Check DC cables for insulation damage. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Isolation Monitor status is not stable — readings are fluctuating. Can't reliably confirm insulation integrity.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon CAN cable. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Cooling Controller CAN communication failed. Station can't manage cooling.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable to cooling controller. Power cycle. Replace CCB if persists. Maps to ccb:can-comms-error.

What It Means: Can't read Power Module Carrier Config. DCC unable to read configuration from power module carrier EEPROM.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Reseat power module carrier. Replace carrier or DCC if persists.

What It Means: Fan speed self-test at 25% — fan-off check failed. Fan should be off but tachometer still shows RPM.
Recommended Action: Check fan control circuit. Replace fan or CCB if persists.

What It Means: Fan speed at 25% command doesn't match expected RPM. Fan is running too fast or too slow.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan if motor is degrading.

What It Means: Failed to read fan fault register during 25% speed test. CAN communication issue with fan controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace CCB if persists.

What It Means: Fan speed self-test at 100% — fan-off check failed.
Recommended Action: Check fan control circuit. Replace fan or CCB.

What It Means: Fan speed at 100% command doesn't match expected RPM.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Replace if motor degrading.

What It Means: Failed to read fan fault register during 100% speed test. (Note: typo in fault name is original.)
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace CCB.

What It Means: Station I/O board CAN communication failed. Can't control or monitor station I/O (door, locks, LEDs).
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable to I/O board. Power cycle. Replace I/O board if persists.

What It Means: Smart Power CAN communication failed. Power management system is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable to Smart Power module. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Not all power module output channels turned on during startup. Some modules may be in fault or not responding.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module status. Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: General fan speed mismatch — actual RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fans for obstructions. Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Failed to access a CAN register on one of the subsystems. Communication or hardware failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CAN cabling. Replace the affected module.

What It Means: No DC power modules detected by the DCC. Station can't charge at all.
Recommended Action: Check power module seating. Check CAN bus. Power cycle. Replace DCC if modules are physically present but not detected.

What It Means: Some power modules are not active — station will charge at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Check which modules are faulted (via DCC logs). Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: Voltage over 50V detected on power module output when it should be in standalone/off mode. Safety hazard — unexpected DC voltage present.
Recommended Action: Do not touch DC components. Power cycle. Replace faulted power module. Check contactors.

What It Means: Unexpected voltage on both power modules' input bus. Station detecting voltage where there shouldn't be any.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Power cycle. Investigate wiring. Replace DCC/power modules if persists.

What It Means: Charge control CAN bus is completely down. All CAN communication for charging control has failed.
Recommended Action: Check CAN bus wiring and termination (120Ω). Power cycle. Replace DCC if bus is physically OK but still down.

What It Means: MCU (Microcontroller Unit) firmware update failed on DCC or subsystem.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: DCC EEPROM read error. Configuration or calibration data couldn't be read from non-volatile memory.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If persists, EEPROM may be corrupted — replace DCC.

What It Means: DCC data partition is full. Logs, session data, or firmware updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Clear logs/old data. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell if needed.

What It Means: DCC temporary partition is full. Firmware updates and temp file operations will fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files. Manual cleanup if persists.

What It Means: 48V voltage rail in the dispenser has failed. Dispenser electronics have no power.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS 48V output. Check wiring to dispenser. Replace DCD board if persists.

What It Means: 32V voltage rail has failed. Motor drive or secondary electronics have no power.
Recommended Action: Check power supply. Replace DCD board if persists.

What It Means: LED indicator has failed. Visual status indicator on dispenser not working.
Recommended Action: Replace LED module. Non-critical — station still charges.

What It Means: Cable retractor motor or motor driver circuit has failed. Cable can't be retracted/extended automatically.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace motor or DCD board if persists.

What It Means: Cable retractor motor has failed completely.
Recommended Action: Replace motor.

What It Means: Cable retractor mechanism is jammed. Motor is trying to move but mechanism is stuck.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstructions (cable tangled, debris). Free mechanism. Replace if hardware is damaged.

What It Means: DC Power Meter lost CAN communication with station controller. DC energy measurement is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to DCPM. Power cycle. Replace DCPM if persists.

What It Means: DCPM firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser AuxPS power supply to cable controller has failed. Cable controller has no power — can't charge.
Recommended Action: Check dispenser AuxPS. Replace dispenser AuxPS module if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser AuxPS power supply to dispenser controller has failed. Entire dispenser is offline.
Recommended Action: Check dispenser AuxPS. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Safety circuit detected pilot signal error in the cable controller via dispenser AuxPS. Control pilot integrity compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check pilot circuit wiring. Replace cable controller or dispenser AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Thermal switch 1 in dispenser has tripped or failed. Overheating in dispenser compartment 1.
Recommended Action: Check dispenser ventilation. Inspect thermal switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Thermal switch 2 tripped or failed.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Inspect switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Thermal switch 3 tripped or failed.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Inspect switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Dispenser controller lost communication with cabinet/Falcon. Dispenser is offline.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet/CAN cable between dispenser and cabinet. Power cycle. Replace dispenser controller if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser controller firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser AuxPS voltage is below warning threshold. Power supply may be degrading.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check AuxPS input power. Replace dispenser AuxPS if voltage drops further.

What It Means: Dispenser AuxPS voltage is above warning threshold. Regulation issue.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace dispenser AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser tilt sensor detected minor tilt. Dispenser may have been bumped or ground is shifting.
Recommended Action: Inspect dispenser mounting. Check if bollard is leaning.

What It Means: Dispenser tilt exceeded safe threshold. Dispenser has been hit or knocked significantly. Potential structural damage.
Recommended Action: Inspect immediately. Check mounting bolts. Check for vehicle impact damage. Station may need to be taken offline for inspection.

What It Means: Dispenser controller board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Dispenser controller board exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace dispenser controller if persists.

What It Means: Dispenser helmet (top cover) has been removed or is not properly seated. Station detects open enclosure.
Recommended Action: Reseat helmet. Check tamper switch. Replace helmet if damaged.

What It Means: Dispenser controller data partition full.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell if needed.

What It Means: Dispenser controller temp partition full.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: Entire power system has failed. Station cannot produce DC power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AC input. Check power modules. Replace DCC or power modules as needed.

What It Means: Paired station (in power-share config) is offline. Power sharing is suspended — this station operates independently.
Recommended Action: Check paired station power and network. Fix paired station.

What It Means: Charge meter (energy measurement) has failed. Billing accuracy affected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace DCPM/ACPM meter if persists.

What It Means: System self-test failed during startup. One or more subsystems didn't pass diagnostics.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Check DCC logs to identify which subsystem failed. Replace faulted component.

What It Means: One or more AC input phases missing. Station not receiving all required phases.
Recommended Action: Check AC breakers. Verify all phases at station input terminals. Contact site electrician.

What It Means: Entire cooling system has failed — no fans, no pump, or both. Station will overheat.
Recommended Action: Check fans, pump, coolant level. Power cycle. Replace cooling components as needed.

What It Means: Cable safety check failed — pre-charge cable integrity test didn't pass. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for damage. Megger test. Replace cable if insulation compromised.

What It Means: CAN bus communication has failed for one or more subsystems.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling and termination (120Ω). Power cycle. Replace DCC if bus wiring is OK.

What It Means: CAN bus is completely down — all CAN communication lost. No subsystem can report status.
Recommended Action: Check CAN bus wiring, connectors, and termination resistors. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: DC contactor/relay is welded shut. Contacts fused together — relay can't open. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not attempt to charge. Power cycle. Replace welded contactor. Inspect for arc damage.

What It Means: Cabinet panel/door is open. Tamper switch detected open enclosure. Station may stop charging for safety.
Recommended Action: Close and secure cabinet panel. Check door latch and tamper switch.

What It Means: Dispenser cabinet physical damage detected (via tilt or tamper sensor).
Recommended Action: Inspect for vehicle impact damage. Check mounting. Take offline if structurally compromised.

What It Means: AC input terminals inside the station are overheating. Loose connections or undersized wire. Fire hazard.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque. Inspect for discoloration/melting. Re-torque or replace terminals.

What It Means: DC power module has failed (duplicate from Batch 4, may appear in different contexts).
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Identify faulted module. Replace power module.

What It Means: Station is reducing output power due to overheating. Self-protecting by limiting current.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Check ambient temp. Ensure airflow is not blocked.

What It Means: Cable type detected doesn't match station configuration. Wrong cable installed.
Recommended Action: Verify correct cable is installed for this station model. Replace cable if wrong.

What It Means: Cooling fan rotor is locked — fan motor seized.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Coolant pump has failed (general legacy fault).
Recommended Action: Replace pump. Check for leaks.

What It Means: Internal power supply voltage has deviated from expected value.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AuxPS. Replace if voltage is unstable.

What It Means: A relay on an unused cable (second connector type) has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor if persists. May not affect primary connector operation.

What It Means: DC contactor is stuck open — can't close to deliver power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Coolant level is critically low. Pump may dry-run. Risk of overheating.
Recommended Action: Top up coolant immediately. Check for leaks. Replace pump if damaged.

What It Means: Coolant leak detected by leak sensor. Safety concern — liquid around electrical components.
Recommended Action: Stop station. Inspect for leak source. Check hoses, fittings, HEX. Replace damaged components.

What It Means: Airflow is blocked — station can't cool effectively.
Recommended Action: Remove obstructions from vents. Check air filters. Clean or replace.

What It Means: Electrical surge detected. Surge protection may have triggered.
Recommended Action: Check surge protectors (SPDs). Replace if triggered. Check site power quality.

What It Means: Emergency stop triggered on cable relay/contactor. Contactors forced open by emergency stop circuit.
Recommended Action: Check emergency stop button. Reset e-stop. Investigate why it was triggered.

What It Means: Falcon detected dispenser tilt exceeds safe threshold. Dispenser has been hit hard.
Recommended Action: Inspect immediately. Check for structural damage. Take offline if needed.

What It Means: Minor dispenser tilt detected. May have been bumped.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Inspect mounting.

What It Means: Falcon detected dispenser helmet (top cover) removed. Open enclosure detected.
Recommended Action: Reseat helmet. Check tamper switch.

What It Means: Falcon controller is overheating. Station controller processor is too hot.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace Falcon if persists.

What It Means: Card reader firmware update initiated by Falcon has failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Falcon lost serial communication with card reader. Card taps won't register.
Recommended Action: Check serial cable between Falcon and card reader. Power cycle. Replace Falcon or card reader if persists.
