What It Means: Falcon input voltage is above expected range. Internal voltage rail too high.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS output. Replace Falcon if voltage regulator is failing.

What It Means: Falcon input voltage is below expected range. May cause instability or reboots.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS output. Check cable from AuxPS to Falcon. Replace Falcon if persists.

What It Means: Last-gasp capacitor error. The capacitor that provides power during shutdown to save state has failed. Station may lose data on unexpected power loss.
Recommended Action: Replace Falcon. Non-critical for charging but data integrity at risk.

What It Means: Screen brightness stuck at max. Backlight control not responding.
Recommended Action: Reboot. Update firmware. Replace display/Falcon if persists. Non-critical.

What It Means: Falcon data partition full. Logs, session data, firmware updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: Falcon temp partition full. Temp file operations and updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: Main cabinet door is open. Tamper/safety switch triggered. Station may stop charging.
Recommended Action: Close and secure main door. Check door latch and tamper switch.

What It Means: Pedestal/dispenser door is open.
Recommended Action: Close pedestal door. Check latch.

What It Means: Fuse safety shield/panel is open. HV fuses may be exposed. Safety concern.
Recommended Action: Close fuse shield immediately. Verify fuses are intact.

What It Means: Pedestal HV (High Voltage) compartment door is open. Live HV components may be exposed. Serious safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Close immediately. Do not touch HV components. Verify HV compartment is secure.

What It Means: Low-voltage surge arrestor 1 has opened/tripped. LV surge protection on circuit 1 is depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace LV surge arrestor 1. Check for surge damage to downstream components.

What It Means: Low-voltage surge arrestor 2 has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace LV surge arrestor 2.

What It Means: High-voltage surge arrestor 1 has tripped. HV surge protection is depleted — station is vulnerable to surges.
Recommended Action: Replace HV surge arrestor 1 immediately. Check for surge damage.

What It Means: High-voltage surge arrestor 2 has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace HV surge arrestor 2 immediately.

What It Means: DC Bus A surge arrestor has tripped. Bus A is unprotected from surges.
Recommended Action: Replace Bus A surge arrestor.

What It Means: DC Bus B surge arrestor has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace Bus B surge arrestor.

What It Means: 48V AuxPS surge arrestor has tripped. AuxPS surge protection depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace 48V AuxPS surge arrestor.

What It Means: Surge arrestor 3 (generic/additional circuit) has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace surge arrestor 3. Check site power quality.

What It Means: Station reducing output power due to high internal temp — self-protecting by limiting charging speed.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system (fans, coolant, HEX). Check for obstructions.

What It Means: AC Board temp exceeded safe threshold. General overheating.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation, ambient temp, cooling components. Power cycle.

What It Means: ACB CPU overheating. Possible cooling failure or excessive load.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Port's AC Board section exceeded 95°C.
Recommended Action: Check head unit seating, blindmate connection. Replace power plate if persists.

What It Means: No power detected to a port. RCCB tripped or voltage too low.
Recommended Action: Confirm jumpers for single circuit. Check wiring. For dual circuit, check breaker. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB not operational. May be temporary during FW update.
Recommended Action: Wait 5 min if FW update in progress. Reboot. Contact engineering if doesn't recover.

What It Means: ACB firmware initialization failure.
Recommended Action: Check ACB menu in chassis-shell. Update SW via NOS. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Internal thread error in ACB firmware.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB firmware stack overflow — memory corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: ACB failed to initialize during startup.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware or replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: ADC error. Voltage/current readings may be unreliable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: CPU encountered fatal hardware or firmware issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Watchdog/DMA error — internal watchdog or memory access failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB self-test failed (CLKERR, FLASHERR, RAMERR, PWRERR, GFCIERR).
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Contact engineering. Replace ACB.

What It Means: GFCI detection circuit failed its diagnostic check.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: General internal power supply issue on ACB.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Flash memory error — firmware storage may be corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: RAM error — working memory failed or corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Internal timing circuit failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: ACB firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: ACB lost CAN bus communication with Falcon/UCB. Latched fault.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable (Pin 10 & 11). Verify impedance (120Ω). Replace Falcon/UCB. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Weld detection circuit not operating OR input voltage below 80V RMS. Does NOT mean relay is welded.
Recommended Action: Check input voltage. If below 200V, schedule site electrical check. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Power plate ID pins have illegal values.
Recommended Action: Check power plate alignment/seating. Verify blindmate. Replace power plate if persists.

What It Means: Relay that should be closed is open. Register bit: RSO.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if doesn't clear.

What It Means: Relay that should be open is closed. Register bit: RSC. Latched fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Test with another vehicle. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Circuit distribution error.
Recommended Action: Contact engineering.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Informational alert about power distribution.
Recommended Action: Monitor. No action unless accompanied by other faults.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Neutral wire error detected.
Recommended Action: Check neutral wiring. Contact engineering.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: Voltage imbalance across phases.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring and phase balance.

What It Means: Power plate overheating at blindmate connection.
Recommended Action: Check head unit seating. Inspect blindmate for debris. Replace power plate if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: AC overcurrent — too much current in AC path.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace power plate/ACB if persists.

What It Means: DC leakage current to ground — safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: DC ground fault — direct current path to ground. Can trigger shunt trip (EU).
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: AC leakage current detected.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and RCCB.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP800 ⚠️ NOT on CP6000
What It Means: One or more AC input phases missing.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring. Verify all phases present.

What It Means: Non-recoverable ground fault. Occurs as relay closes (NA only). Auto-clears when EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for EV disconnect. If recurring, replace ACB.

What It Means: Relay coil GPIO in unexpected state. Register bit: RLYP.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if doesn't clear.

What It Means: Ground monitoring circuit itself has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Ground monitoring detected actual ground issue.
Recommended Action: Check station grounding. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Recoverable ground fault — may clear after EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for session end. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: General ground fault — current flowing to ground unexpectedly.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check wiring and grounding. Replace ACB if persists.

Model(s): CPE250, CPE280, CP6000 — EU only (NA has no RCCBs)
What It Means: Shunt trip activated. If right port faults — entire station turns off.
Recommended Action: Check RCCB. Rearm if tripped. Investigate root cause.

What It Means: Differential current sensing circuit issue. Register bit: GFERR.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. RMA head unit if doesn't resolve. Falcon can be kept.

What It Means: Same as ground-error. Register bit: GFERR.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. RMA head unit if doesn't resolve. Falcon can be kept.

What It Means: Station drawing more current than allowed.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: AC-specific overcurrent through charging circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and breaker. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Soft GFCI trip — minor ground fault.
Recommended Action: Usually self-clearing. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Hard GFCI trip — significant ground fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Ground monitoring detected fault.
Recommended Action: Check station grounding. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: GFCI self-test error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Relay stuck open. Can't energize charging cable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Stack overflow in ACC firmware.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Relay stuck closed. Safety issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: UART serial communication error.
Recommended Action: Check cable connections. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Hard ground fault detection circuit failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: GFCI sensing coil failed.
Recommended Action: Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Current sensing coil error.
Recommended Action: Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Voltage fluctuations on AC input.
Recommended Action: Check site power quality. Replace ACC if site power is OK.

What It Means: AC voltage out of acceptable range.
Recommended Action: Check incoming voltage. Verify breaker and wiring.

What It Means: J1772 pilot signal voltage incorrect.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit wiring. Test with another vehicle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Charging state machine timed out.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. If recurring, escalate to engineering.

What It Means: ACC self-test didn't complete in time.
Recommended Action: Retry self-test. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: AC input frequency out of range.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Verify generator frequency if on backup power.

What It Means: Ground monitor error test failure. Often seen with zero energy dispense.
Recommended Action: Resend configurations. Reboot. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: CPU internal registers failed check.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: CPU instruction pointer corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: RAM error — working memory corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACC board.

What It Means: Flash memory error — firmware storage corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: CPU temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation. Usually self-clearing.

What It Means: Input cable temp elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Ensure proper torque on terminals.

What It Means: Output cable temp elevated but not critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cable for damage.

What It Means: CPU exceeded critical temperature. Charging stops.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace ACC if persists.

What It Means: Input cable exceeded critical temp. Potential fire risk.
Recommended Action: Immediately check cable and connections. Inspect for loose terminals.

What It Means: Output cable exceeded critical temp.
Recommended Action: Check cable for damage. Check connector. Reduce charging current if possible.

What It Means: AC Power Meter lost CAN communication.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to ACPM. Power cycle. Replace ACPM if persists.

What It Means: ACPM firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS lost CAN communication with DCC.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: AuxPS firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Fan 1 RPM doesn't match expected speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan if persists.

What It Means: Board temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation and ambient temp.

What It Means: Board temp reached critical level.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Main 48V output below expected but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. May indicate degrading AuxPS.

What It Means: Main 48V output above expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if voltage stays high.

What It Means: External 48V rail below expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check load on external rail.

What It Means: External 48V rail above expected.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Main output drawing more current than expected.
Recommended Action: Check connected components. Monitor.

What It Means: External output overcurrent caused shutdown.
Recommended Action: Check what's connected to ext output. Power cycle. Replace AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: Lost AC input power — main AC feed interrupted.
Recommended Action: Check breakers. Verify AC supply to station.

What It Means: Lost DC input — DC bus feeding AuxPS is down.
Recommended Action: Check DC connections. Power cycle.

What It Means: DC input low but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check power source.

What It Means: DC input above expected range.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check power source.

What It Means: AC input low — site may have voltage drop.
Recommended Action: Check AC supply voltage. Verify site wiring.

What It Means: AC input above expected — site voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Check AC supply. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if failed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 1 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if needed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 2 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace AuxPS if needed.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 shorted — false high-temp readings.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 2 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace AuxPS.

What It Means: NTC sensor 0 reading inconsistent.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace AuxPS if readings unreliable.

What It Means: Thermal switch 1 has opened — overheating detected in the FDC. Power delivery may be interrupted.
Recommended Action: Inspect FDC for heat source. Check ventilation. Replace thermal switch if failed.

What It Means: Thermal switch 2 has opened — overheating in a second zone of the FDC.
Recommended Action: Inspect FDC. Check ventilation. Replace thermal switch if failed.

What It Means: FDC disconnect switch has been activated. Power to the station is intentionally cut — someone pulled the disconnect.
Recommended Action: Check if disconnect was pulled intentionally. Reset disconnect. Verify station resumes.

What It Means: Bottom RTD temperature sensor is shorted — giving false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or FDC board.

What It Means: Bottom RTD sensor is open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring. Reconnect or replace.

What It Means: MDS (Module Distribution System) RTD sensor shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or FDC board.

What It Means: MDS RTD sensor open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring.

What It Means: Top RTD sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or FDC board.

What It Means: Top RTD sensor open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring.

What It Means: FDC lost CAN bus communication with station controller. FDC status is unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to FDC. Power cycle. Replace FDC board if persists.

What It Means: Vicor DC-DC converter in FDC is above warning temp. Converter module is running hot.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Vicor DC-DC converter exceeded critical temp — shut down. Internal power conversion lost.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace Vicor module if persists.

What It Means: Top section of FDC above warning temp.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Top section exceeded critical temp — FDC shut down that zone.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Inspect for heat source. Power cycle.

What It Means: Top section of FDC below warning temp — too cold. Heater may have failed in cold climate.
Recommended Action: Check heater. May need cold-climate kit.

What It Means: MDS zone above warning temp.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation and airflow around MDS.

What It Means: MDS zone exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Inspect MDS area. Check fans and ventilation. Power cycle.

What It Means: Bottom section above warning temp.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Bottom section exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation and heat sources. Power cycle.

What It Means: FDC board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: FDC board exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace FDC board if persists.

What It Means: FDC firmware has failed — board not executing code properly.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace FDC board if persists.

What It Means: Mixing fan drawing overcurrent — motor may be failing or obstructed.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace mixing fan.

What It Means: Mixing fan has open circuit — not powered. Wiring disconnected or motor coil broken.
Recommended Action: Check fan wiring. Replace mixing fan.

What It Means: MDS fan drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace MDS fan.

What It Means: MDS fan has open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace MDS fan.

What It Means: External heatsink fan overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: External heatsink fan open circuit — not powered.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan.

What It Means: Primary Proton (power module) fan overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Primary Proton fan open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan.

What It Means: Optional/secondary Proton fan overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Optional Proton fan open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan.

What It Means: Stirring fan (air circulation) overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Stirring fan open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan.

What It Means: SSLAN (Safety System LAN) load switch fault. Power to safety network communication has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check FDC board. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Proton (power module) load switch fault. Power delivery to Proton module interrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check FDC board. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Holster load switch fault. Power to cable holster heater/sensor has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check FDC board. Replace if persists.

What It Means: CCB (Cooling Control Board) load switch fault. Power to cooling controller interrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check FDC board. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Contactor load switch fault. Power to DC contactors interrupted — can't switch contactors. Charging impossible.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check FDC board. Replace if persists.

What It Means: DC input contactor A is stuck open. Can't close to connect power modules to DC bus.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor A.

What It Means: DC input contactor B is stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor B.

What It Means: DC input contactor A is stuck closed/welded. Can't disconnect power modules from DC bus. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not service while energized. Power cycle. Replace contactor A. Check for arc damage.

What It Means: DC input contactor B is stuck closed/welded.
Recommended Action: Do not service while energized. Replace contactor B.

What It Means: Power Limited station derating because a thermal switch opened. Charging continues at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Check thermal switch. Fix thermal issue first.

What It Means: Primary ACD (AC Disconnect) thermal switch has opened. Overheating near AC disconnect — power interrupted.
Recommended Action: Inspect AC disconnect area. Check for loose AC connections. Replace thermal switch if failed.

What It Means: Optional/secondary ACD thermal switch has opened.
Recommended Action: Inspect AC disconnect area. Check connections. Replace switch if failed.

What It Means: Omni port adapter (connector adapter for CHAdeMO/CCS) has been lost — station can't detect the adapter.
Recommended Action: Check adapter connection. Reseat adapter. Replace adapter if persists.

What It Means: Adapter lock motor has failed. Adapter can't be locked/unlocked automatically.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace omni port controller or motor.

What It Means: Cable holster lock motor has failed. Cable holster can't lock/unlock.
Recommended Action: Check motor. Replace omni port controller or motor.

What It Means: 12V supply to omni port controller below warning threshold.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check 12V power supply.

What It Means: 5V supply below warning threshold.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check 5V regulator.

What It Means: Omni port controller firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Omni port controller lost communication with station controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace controller if persists.

What It Means: Insulation monitoring device detected isolation failure. DC system insulation resistance is below safe threshold. Safety hazard — current may leak to ground.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect DC cables for insulation damage. Check connector. Replace IMD if persists.

What It Means: DC isolation fault detected by IsoMon. Insulation between DC output and ground is compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DC cables. Replace IsoMon if persists. Maps to isomon:isolation-fault.

What It Means: IsoMon self-test failed. Can't verify isolation monitoring is working.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace IsoMon if persists. Maps to isomon:self-test-failed.

What It Means: DC contactor 1 is stuck closed/welded. Can't open. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not service while energized. Replace contactor 1. Maps to isomon:relay-stuck-closed.

What It Means: DC contactor 2 is stuck closed/welded.
Recommended Action: Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: DC contactor 3 (precharge) is stuck closed/welded.
Recommended Action: Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: DC contactor 1 stuck open — can't close to deliver power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 1. Maps to isomon:relay-stuck-open.

What It Means: DC contactor 2 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: DC contactor 3 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Emergency shutdown triggered. All contactors opened, charging stopped immediately. Could be from e-stop button, software trigger, or safety system.
Recommended Action: Check e-stop button. Reset. Investigate root cause. Maps to isomon:emergency-shutdown.

What It Means: General software error on DCC/IsoMon. Firmware encountered an unrecoverable error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace DCC/IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: DC bus bar 1 is overheating. High-current copper bar inside station is too hot. Loose bolted connection or overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check bus bar bolted connections. Re-torque. Inspect for discoloration.

What It Means: DC bus bar 2 overheating.
Recommended Action: Check bolted connections. Re-torque.

What It Means: DC bus bar 3 overheating.
Recommended Action: Check bolted connections. Re-torque.

What It Means: Tamper detection triggered on IsoMon module. Someone may have opened or manipulated the IsoMon enclosure.
Recommended Action: Check IsoMon physical integrity. Reseat IsoMon. Investigate tamper source.

What It Means: IsoMon lost CAN communication with station controller. Isolation monitoring is offline — station can't verify DC safety.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable to IsoMon. Power cycle. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Isolation fault detected. DC insulation resistance below safe threshold.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect DC cables/connector for insulation damage. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: IsoMon self-test failed. Isolation monitoring can't be trusted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: IsoMon detected a relay/contactor stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Replace affected contactor. Check for arc damage.

What It Means: IsoMon detected a relay/contactor stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Emergency shutdown triggered via IsoMon. All contactors opened.
Recommended Action: Check e-stop button. Reset. Investigate root cause.

What It Means: Entire power system has failed. No DC power available for charging.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules. Replace DCC/power modules as needed.

What It Means: Vehicle sent an emergency stop request. Vehicle's BMS triggered an immediate charging stop.
Recommended Action: No station action needed — vehicle initiated. If recurring with same vehicle, vehicle may have an issue. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Cable insulation check detected short circuit. DC cable or connector has failed insulation test. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for physical damage. Megger test. Replace cable if insulation compromised.

What It Means: Cable check procedure encountered an error — test couldn't complete.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Retry session. If persists, check IsoMon and cable controller.

What It Means: IMD (Insulation Monitoring Device) functional check failed. Can't verify the isolation monitor is working.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace IMD/IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Proximity Pilot (PP) signal is absent. Station can't detect cable connection. Cable may not be plugged in or PP circuit is broken.
Recommended Action: Check connector seating. Check PP wiring. Replace cable if PP circuit is open.

What It Means: PP signal circuit is open. Cable detected but PP resistor is missing or open.
Recommended Action: Check PP resistor in connector. Replace cable if needed.

What It Means: PP signal was present but lost during session. Cable may have been partially unplugged.
Recommended Action: Check connector latch. Reseat cable. Check PP wiring.

What It Means: Control Pilot (CP) signal is absent. Station can't communicate with vehicle via pilot. No handshake possible.
Recommended Action: Check CP circuit wiring. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: CP signal was present but lost during session. Communication with vehicle interrupted mid-charge.
Recommended Action: Check connector. Retry session. Replace cable controller if recurring.

What It Means: CP signal voltage is invalid — not at a recognized IEC/SAE state.
Recommended Action: Check CP circuit. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: CP signal is shorted to ground. Pilot circuit has a short. Charging impossible and potential safety issue.
Recommended Action: Check CP wiring for shorts. Replace cable or cable controller.

What It Means: Cable connector not fully inserted into vehicle inlet.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector. Check connector latch.

What It Means: Power interconnect between station and power system is unsafe. Hardware configuration or wiring issue.
Recommended Action: Check power interconnect wiring. Contact engineering. Do not attempt to charge.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during cable safety check. Insulation failure detected before charging started.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable insulation. Megger test. Replace cable if compromised.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during active charging session. Immediate safety stop. Current leaking to ground.
Recommended Action: Charging stops automatically. Inspect cable and DC path. Replace cable/IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Tamper detection triggered. Someone has opened an enclosure or tampered with safety-critical components.
Recommended Action: Inspect station for unauthorized access. Check tamper switches. Reseat covers.

What It Means: Peer station (in power-share) has been locked out. This station is affected by the peer's lockout.
Recommended Action: Fix peer station fault first. Clear lockout on peer.

What It Means: Peer station is in an unsafe state. This station won't share power with an unsafe peer.
Recommended Action: Fix peer station. Investigate peer fault.

What It Means: Jupiter lost communication with the station controller (Falcon/CPNK). Session management is offline.
Recommended Action: Check internal communication. Power cycle. Replace Falcon if persists.

What It Means: Vehicle reported a voltage outside the station's supported range during CCS negotiation.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module voltage range.

What It Means: Vehicle's precharge voltage doesn't match expected value. Station can't safely close contactors because voltage mismatch could cause inrush current.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check voltage measurement. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Overcurrent detected during DC charging. Current exceeded safe limits. Contactors opened immediately.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules and current sensors. Replace if persists.

What It Means: DC output voltage exceeded safe limits. Safety hazard — vehicle battery could be damaged.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module voltage regulation. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Negative voltage detected on DC output — polarity issue. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check wiring polarity. Replace power module or contactors if persists.

What It Means: Entire power module cluster is in an unsafe state. Multiple modules reporting issues simultaneously.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all power modules. Replace cluster if persists. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Excess capacitance detected on the DC output. Vehicle or cable has too much capacitance — can't safely precharge.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. May be vehicle-specific. Escalate if seen with multiple vehicles.

What It Means: Eichrecht-certified meter reading doesn't match expected value. Billing integrity is compromised. EU legal compliance issue.
Recommended Action: Check meter calibration. Replace meter if mismatch persists. Station may need to be taken offline for compliance.

What It Means: Cryptographic signature verification failed on meter data. Data integrity can't be proven. Eichrecht compliance failure.
Recommended Action: Check meter module. Replace meter. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Power system is locked out — requires manual intervention to clear. Multiple faults accumulated.
Recommended Action: Clear lockout via installer app or console. Fix underlying faults first.

What It Means: Power system is completely inoperable. No modules can produce power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all modules. Replace DCC and modules as needed.

What It Means: Failed to reserve power modules for a charging session. All modules may be in use or faulted.
Recommended Action: Retry session. Check module status.

What It Means: Failed to set charging parameters on power system. Voltage/current targets couldn't be configured.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Power system failed to energize — couldn't start delivering power. Contactors may not have closed or modules didn't output.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check contactors and power modules.

What It Means: Power system failed to de-energize — couldn't stop delivering power safely. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle immediately. Check contactors. Replace if stuck.

What It Means: Power system couldn't meet the current demand. Modules not responding to current commands.
Recommended Action: Check power modules. Power cycle. Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: Power module reservation was lost during session. Module was taken away or faulted mid-charge.
Recommended Action: Session may restart at lower power. Check modules.

What It Means: Communication with power system lost entirely. Jupiter can't control power delivery.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet to power system. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Power interconnect configuration is invalid. Hardware wiring or config doesn't match expected topology.
Recommended Action: Check power interconnect. Verify configuration. Contact engineering.

What It Means: Energy meter is offline. Can't measure energy delivered — billing will be inaccurate. Station may stop charging (especially Eichrecht markets).
Recommended Action: Check meter CAN/comms. Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: UL (Underwriters Laboratories) safety stop triggered. A UL-certified safety check has failed. Regulatory safety event.
Recommended Action: Investigate immediately. Check all safety systems. Do not bypass. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Ambient temperature reading is invalid — sensor may be failed or disconnected. Station can't factor ambient temp into thermal decisions.
Recommended Action: Check ambient temp sensor. Replace sensor.

What It Means: This specific unit (port/dispenser) is locked out. Requires manual clearance.
Recommended Action: Clear lockout via installer app or console. Fix underlying faults first.

What It Means: KCB detected overcurrent. Station drawing more current than allowed.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace KCB if persists.

What It Means: KCB board temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: KCB exceeded critical temperature. Charging stops.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace KCB if persists.

What It Means: Last-gasp capacitor error. Capacitor that saves state during power loss has failed.
Recommended Action: Replace KCB. Non-critical for charging but data at risk.

What It Means: KCB data partition full. Logs and session data can't be stored.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: KCB temp partition full. Firmware updates and temp operations fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: LCC lost CAN communication. Cable temperature/safety monitoring is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable to LCC. Power cycle. Replace LCC if persists.

What It Means: LCC status output is low — safety interlock signal is not asserted. LCC is signaling "not OK."
Recommended Action: Check LCC board. Power cycle. Replace LCC if persists.

What It Means: LCC controller has failed completely. Cable monitoring and protection is offline.
Recommended Action: Replace LCC board.

What It Means: Positive connector contact has exceeded critical temperature. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector pins. Check coolant flow. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Negative connector contact exceeded critical temp.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector. Check coolant flow. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Positive cable body exceeded critical temp — charging stopped. Cooling failure or cable damage.
Recommended Action: Check coolant system. Inspect cable. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Negative cable body exceeded critical temp.
Recommended Action: Check coolant system. Inspect cable. Replace if persists.

What It Means: General cable overtemp — cable body temperature exceeded critical threshold.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Inspect cable. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Connector housing exceeded critical temp. Housing is dangerously hot.
Recommended Action: Inspect housing. Check coolant flow. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Coolant leakage detected inside cable. Safety hazard — liquid near electrical components.
Recommended Action: Stop station. Inspect cable for leaks. Check fittings. Replace cable.

What It Means: Major impact/shock detected on cable. Cable may be physically damaged.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable and connector thoroughly. Replace if damaged.

What It Means: Cable temperature sensor has failed. Can't monitor cable temp — safety blind spot.
Recommended Action: Replace cable or LCC.

What It Means: Housing temp sensor failed.
Recommended Action: Replace cable or LCC.

What It Means: Leakage sensor failed. Can't detect coolant leaks.
Recommended Action: Replace cable or LCC.

What It Means: Shock/impact sensor failed. Can't detect cable damage from impacts.
Recommended Action: Replace cable or LCC.

What It Means: Positive contact temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connector. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Negative contact temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check connector.

What It Means: Positive cable body temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Negative cable body temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: General cable temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling system.

What It Means: Housing temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check coolant flow.

What It Means: Temperature difference between pos and neg cable bodies exceeds threshold.
Recommended Action: Check coolant distribution. Inspect cable.

What It Means: Contact temperature difference exceeds threshold. One pin may have poor contact.
Recommended Action: Check connector alignment. Clean pins.

What It Means: Leakage sensor detected minor moisture. Small leak may be developing.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Inspect cable fittings.

What It Means: Minor impact detected on cable.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Inspect cable for damage.

What It Means: Downlight LED has failed. Visual illumination for the charging area is not working.
Recommended Action: Replace LED module. Non-critical — station still charges.

What It Means: Downlight LED drawing overcurrent. LED driver or LED module may be failing.
Recommended Action: Check LED wiring. Replace LED module or driver. Non-critical.

What It Means: RGB LED module has failed. Status ring or indicator light not working. Station still charges.
Recommended Action: Replace LED module. Non-critical.

What It Means: RGB LED drawing overcurrent — LED driver or LED strip may be short-circuiting.
Recommended Action: Check LED wiring. Replace LED module if persists. Non-critical.

What It Means: Line Energy Monitor lost CAN communication with station controller. AC input energy measurement is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to LEM. Power cycle. Replace LEM if persists.

What It Means: MCB overcurrent detected. Controller board drawing too much current.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCB if persists.

What It Means: MCB temperature above warning threshold but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: MCB exceeded critical temperature. Board may shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation and ambient temp. Power cycle. Replace MCB if persists.

What It Means: Last-gasp capacitor error. The cap that saves state during power loss has failed. Data integrity at risk on unexpected power loss.
Recommended Action: Replace MCB. Non-critical for charging.

What It Means: MCB data partition full. Logs, session data, firmware updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: MCB temp partition full. Firmware updates and temp file operations will fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: General overcurrent detected. Station drawing more current than allowed.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: AC-specific overcurrent through charging circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and breaker. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Soft GFCI trip — minor ground fault detected. Session may stop temporarily.
Recommended Action: Usually self-clearing. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Hard GFCI trip — significant ground fault. Station goes to fault state.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Ground monitoring detected fault — earth reference abnormal.
Recommended Action: Check station grounding. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: GFCI self-test error — ground fault detection circuit failed its own diagnostic.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Relay that should be closed is stuck open. Can't energize charging cable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Breakaway connector has been triggered — cable was forcefully pulled and the breakaway mechanism activated. Physical damage likely.
Recommended Action: Inspect breakaway connector. Reassemble if undamaged. Replace cable/connector if damaged.

What It Means: General hardware fault on MCC board.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC.

What It Means: Relay that should be open is stuck closed. Safety issue — voltage may be present on connector.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: SPI bus communication error between MCC and other components. Internal data link failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Hard ground fault detection circuit itself has failed. Safety system can't function.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC.

What It Means: Current sensing coil error — charging current measurement is faulty.
Recommended Action: Replace MCC.

What It Means: Voltage fluctuations on AC input — unstable grid supply.
Recommended Action: Check site power quality. Replace MCC if site power is OK.

What It Means: AC voltage out of acceptable range.
Recommended Action: Check incoming voltage. Verify breaker and wiring.

What It Means: Control pilot signal voltage incorrect. Communication with vehicle abnormal.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit wiring. Test with another vehicle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: MCC self-test didn't complete within expected time.
Recommended Action: Retry self-test. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Flash memory error — firmware storage corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: DC isolation fault detected by MDS. Insulation resistance between DC output and ground is too low. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check DC cables. Megger test. Replace cables or MDS if persists.

What It Means: Relay bank 1 opened abnormally — unexpected contactor opening during operation.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 1 coil drive. Power cycle. Replace contactor if persists.

What It Means: Relay bank 2 opened abnormally.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 2. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Relay bank 3 opened abnormally.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 3. Power cycle. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Relay bank 1 opened during a critical phase (e.g., active charging). May cause arc damage.
Recommended Action: Inspect contactor 1 for arc damage. Replace contactor. Check coil drive circuit.

What It Means: Relay bank 2 opened during critical phase.
Recommended Action: Inspect contactor 2. Replace.

What It Means: Relay bank 3 opened during critical phase.
Recommended Action: Inspect contactor 3. Replace.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 1 stuck closed — can't open. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor. Check for welding.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 1 stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 2 stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 2 stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 3 stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 3 stuck closed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 1 stuck open — can't close to deliver power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 1 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 2 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 2 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Positive contactor bank 3 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Negative contactor bank 3 stuck open.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: MDS lost CAN communication with station controller. Contactor status unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace MDS if persists.

What It Means: MDS firmware has failed. Board cannot operate.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace MDS if persists.

What It Means: MDS board temp above warning but below critical.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: MDS exceeded critical temp — shut down. Contactors may be forced open.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace MDS if persists.

What It Means: Thermal switch 1 on MDS has tripped. Overheating in contactor bank 1 area.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 1 connections. Inspect for loose terminals.

What It Means: Thermal switch 2 tripped.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 2 connections.

What It Means: Thermal switch 3 tripped.
Recommended Action: Check contactor 3 connections.

What It Means: MDS can't measure charging voltage. Voltage feedback is lost.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MDS voltage sensing circuit.

What It Means: MDS can't measure charging current. Current feedback lost. Safety concern.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MDS or current sensor.

What It Means: Charging meter integrated into MDS has failed. Billing accuracy affected and safety monitoring degraded.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MDS meter module.

What It Means: Power Limited station is derating because MDS thermal switch tripped. Charging at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Fix root cause of overheating. Check contactor connections and ventilation.

What It Means: Contactor bank 1 is welded — contacts fused together. Safety hazard — relay can't open.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Replace contactor 1. Inspect for arc damage.

What It Means: Contactor bank 2 welded.
Recommended Action: Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Contactor bank 3 welded.
Recommended Action: Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Current sensor reads non-zero when no current should be flowing. Sensor offset or calibration error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Recalibrate. Replace current sensor or MDS if persists.

What It Means: Hardware-level overcurrent detected by MDS. Current exceeded hardware protection threshold.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check for short circuits. Replace MDS if persists.

What It Means: Communication with secondary Proton (power module) lost. Power capacity reduced.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet to secondary Proton. Power cycle. Replace Proton if persists.

What It Means: Secondary Proton's safety relay has opened. Power module is in safety shutdown.
Recommended Action: Check secondary Proton for faults. Power cycle. Replace Proton if persists.

What It Means: Combined output from multiple power modules has an error. Voltage or current mismatch between parallel modules.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all power module outputs. Replace faulted module.

What It Means: Excessive voltage imbalance between parallel power module outputs. Risk of circulating currents.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module calibration. Replace faulted module.

What It Means: Meter module lost communication with station controller. Energy measurement offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: Meter firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Meter board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: Meter board exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: Meter can't accurately measure charging voltage. Billing and safety affected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: Meter can't accurately measure charging current.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: General meter error — energy measurement is unreliable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: LV surge arrestor in pedestal has tripped. LV protection depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace surge arrestor.

What It Means: Thermal switch in pedestal has tripped. Internal overheating detected.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Inspect for heat source.

What It Means: Shunt trip mechanism failed to operate. Safety disconnect didn't work. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect shunt trip circuit. Replace shunt trip device.

What It Means: ADC read failure on PBC. Analog sensor readings unavailable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace PBC if persists.

What It Means: Temperature reading is invalid — sensor returning out-of-range values.
Recommended Action: Check temp sensor connections. Replace sensor or PBC if persists.

What It Means: Pedestal fan drawing overcurrent. Motor may be failing.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan if motor degrading.

What It Means: Pedestal fan open circuit — not powered.
Recommended Action: Check fan wiring. Replace fan.

What It Means: Pedestal fan 1 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Pedestal fan 2 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor. Replace if degrading.

What It Means: Dry zone ambient RTD sensor disconnected. Can't monitor ambient temp in dry compartment.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: Dry zone ambient RTD sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or PBC.

What It Means: Pedestal base door is open. Tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door. Check latch.

What It Means: Pedestal tilt exceeded safe threshold. Bollard has been hit or is leaning. Structural concern.
Recommended Action: Inspect immediately. Check mounting bolts. Check for vehicle impact damage.

What It Means: Minor pedestal tilt detected. May have been bumped.
Recommended Action: Inspect mounting. Monitor.

What It Means: PBC temperature above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: PBC exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace PBC if persists.

What It Means: 48V logic supply to PBC has been lost. PBC has no power — all pedestal functions offline.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS 48V output. Check cabling to PBC. Replace PBC or AuxPS if persists.

What It Means: 48V supply to PBC is above expected voltage.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check AuxPS regulation.

What It Means: PBC lost CAN communication with station controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace PBC if persists.

What It Means: AC surge arrestor in pedestal has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace AC surge arrestor.

What It Means: DC Bus A surge arrestor in pedestal has tripped. Bus A unprotected.
Recommended Action: Replace Bus A surge arrestor immediately.

What It Means: DC Bus B surge arrestor has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace Bus B surge arrestor immediately.

What It Means: Station derating due to thermal switch trip in pedestal. Charging at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Inspect for heat source. Fix root cause.

What It Means: Pedestal wet box front door is open. High-current/cooling compartment exposed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door. Check latch.

What It Means: Pedestal wet box rear door is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door.

What It Means: Pedestal dry box front door is open. Electronics compartment exposed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door.

What It Means: AC section thermal switch tripped. Overheating in AC compartment.
Recommended Action: Check AC wiring. Inspect terminals for loose connections.

What It Means: DC section thermal switch tripped.
Recommended Action: Check DC wiring and contactor connections.

What It Means: DC Bus A thermal switch tripped.
Recommended Action: Check Bus A connections.

What It Means: DC Bus B thermal switch tripped.
Recommended Action: Check Bus B connections.

What It Means: Pedestal fan 1 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Pedestal fan 2 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan 1 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan 1. Monitor.

What It Means: Fan 2 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan 2. Monitor.

What It Means: PBC data partition full.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot.

What It Means: PBC temp partition full.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: Power module "strike out" — module has faulted too many times and is permanently disabled until reset. Module won't participate in charging.
Recommended Action: Power cycle to reset strike count. If recurs, replace power module.

What It Means: AC input frequency is outside acceptable range (not 50/60Hz). Power modules can't sync.
Recommended Action: Check site power. If on generator, verify frequency stability.

What It Means: Ambient temperature around power modules is above warning. Station in hot environment.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Station may derate. No hardware fix — environmental.

What It Means: Rectifier (AC-to-DC converter stage) temperature above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling. May derate.

What It Means: Charger (DC-DC converter stage) temperature above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: AC phase rotation is incorrect. Phases are connected in wrong order (e.g., ABC vs ACB).
Recommended Action: Site electrician must swap two of the three phases at the input.

What It Means: Temperature sensor inside power module has failed completely. Module can't monitor its own temperature.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Excessive humidity detected inside the station. Risk of condensation on electronics.
Recommended Action: Check seals, cable glands, door gaskets. Inspect for water ingress. Add desiccant or dehumidifier if in humid climate.

What It Means: Condensation detected or conditions are favorable for condensation.
Recommended Action: Monitor humidity. Check seals. Ensure ventilation.

What It Means: DC input to power module is below minimum voltage. DC link not at expected level.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Check rectifier stage. Power cycle.

What It Means: General temperature warning from power module.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling system.

What It Means: Power module has reset itself. Informational — may indicate transient fault or watchdog trigger.
Recommended Action: Monitor. If frequent, investigate root cause.

What It Means: Power module firmware executed an illegal instruction. Firmware corruption or hardware fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Non-Maskable Interrupt triggered in power module. Critical hardware or firmware error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Power module processor has reset. May be watchdog or transient glitch.
Recommended Action: Monitor. If frequent, replace power module.

What It Means: Software fault in power module firmware. Unhandled exception or assertion failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC bus discharge failed — voltage on DC bus didn't bleed down after charging stopped. Safety hazard — high voltage may persist.
Recommended Action: Do not open station. Wait and retry. Check discharge resistor circuit. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Multiple temperature sensors on power module disagree. One sensor may be drifting.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace power module if readings diverge significantly.

What It Means: Power module overheating — current automatically reduced (derating). Self-protection active.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Station will recover when temp drops.

What It Means: AC input voltage is above maximum — site voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Check site power. May need utility investigation. Power module will shut down if sustained.

What It Means: AC input voltage is below minimum — site voltage too low.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Check breakers. Verify wiring.

What It Means: AC overcurrent detected by power module. Drawing more current than rated.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Check for short circuit. Replace power module if internal fault.

What It Means: DC output voltage exceeded maximum. Vehicle may have pushed voltage back.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module regulation. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC output current exceeded maximum. Overcurrent protection triggered.
Recommended Action: Check for short circuit in cable or contactor. Replace power module if internal fault.

What It Means: DC link (intermediate bus between rectifier and inverter) voltage is too high. Regenerative energy from vehicle or regulation failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module. Replace if persists.

What It Means: DC link voltage is too low. Rectifier not providing sufficient voltage.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace power module if rectifier stage failed.

What It Means: General charger (DC-DC stage) fault. Power module can't convert to required output voltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module.

What It Means: Rectifier stage (AC-DC) has shut down due to fault. No DC link voltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AC input. Replace power module if rectifier failed.

What It Means: Charger stage (DC-DC) has shut down. No output power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module.

What It Means: Generic communication error from power module. CAN or serial link interrupted.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Internal relay in power module has failed.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Charger stage failed to reach target voltage during startup. Output voltage didn't ramp as expected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: DC output voltage is below target. Vehicle may not accept charge at this voltage.
Recommended Action: Check power module. Try different vehicle. Replace module if regulation failed.

What It Means: Power module output channel A is not ready. Module failed startup check on channel A.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Power module output channel B is not ready.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Charger internal switching frequency is below expected. Control loop issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module.

What It Means: Range switch (voltage range selector) failed to switch. Module can't change voltage range.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module.

What It Means: DC output undervoltage caused module shutdown. Voltage dropped below minimum safe level.
Recommended Action: Check power module. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Ambient temperature exceeded maximum — power module shut down to prevent damage.
Recommended Action: Environmental condition. Station may need shade or relocation. Wait for temp to drop.

What It Means: Transformer temperature exceeded maximum — power module shut down. Internal transformer overheated.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Power cycle. Replace power module if transformer is damaged.

What It Means: LLC resonant converter primary overcurrent detected inside power module. Switching stage is drawing excessive current.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module.

What It Means: AC input current imbalance between phases. One phase is drawing significantly more/less than others.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring. Check for failed rectifier leg. Replace power module if internal fault.

What It Means: DCC sent an invalid voltage target to the power module. Software or communication error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Escalate to engineering if persists.

What It Means: Emergency Power Off (EPO) activated. E-stop button pressed or EPO circuit triggered. All power shut down immediately.
Recommended Action: Check E-stop button. Reset EPO. Investigate why it was triggered.

What It Means: DC link voltage failed to reach target during startup. Precharge circuit or rectifier may have failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge resistor and relay. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: AC input harmonic distortion exceeds limits. Power module generating too many harmonics on the grid. May violate utility requirements.
Recommended Action: Check site power quality. Replace power module if internal PFC (Power Factor Correction) is failing.

What It Means: Charger (DC-DC stage) timed out during startup. Didn't reach operating state in time.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Output relay failed to close. Power module can't connect its output to the DC bus.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace output relay or power module.

What It Means: DC link precharge circuit fault. Precharge resistor or relay failed — can't safely bring up DC link voltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge components. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Power module EEPROM error. Configuration, calibration, or serial number data corrupted or unreadable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if EEPROM is permanently failed.

What It Means: PLD (Programmable Logic Device) firmware version conflict. FPGA/CPLD version doesn't match expected version for this firmware.
Recommended Action: Update power module firmware. Replace module if incompatible hardware.

What It Means: Power module board ID is invalid. Hardware identification pins or EEPROM data don't match any known configuration.
Recommended Action: Check module hardware. Replace power module if ID can't be resolved.

What It Means: Internal 24V power rail has failed in the power module. Control circuits have no power.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Internal 5V power rail has failed. Digital logic and sensors have no power.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Internal 3.3V power rail has failed. Processor and communication circuits have no power.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Current sensing circuit has failed inside power module. Module can't measure its own output current. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Power module failed to boot. Firmware didn't load or processor didn't start.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Internal RTOS task error in power module firmware. A real-time task crashed or exceeded its timing deadline.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Gate driver power supply fault. The power supply that drives the switching transistors (IGBTs/MOSFETs) has failed. Module can't switch.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Converter enable signal fault. The LLC or PFC converter couldn't be enabled — interlock or safety check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Programmable device (FPGA/CPLD) check failed. Logic device configuration is corrupted or version is wrong. (Note: typo "Progammable" is original.)
Recommended Action: Update firmware. Replace power module if FPGA is damaged.

What It Means: Converter calibration procedure failed. Voltage/current sensors couldn't be calibrated to reference values.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Retry calibration. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Power module is not responding to CAN commands from DCC. Module is present but not communicating.
Recommended Action: Check CAN bus. Power cycle. Reseat module. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Power module is communicating but not in ready state. Failed startup or stuck in fault recovery.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check module status via DCC logs. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Power module is physically installed but not detected on CAN bus. Hardware or bus wiring issue.
Recommended Action: Check CAN connections. Reseat module. Check for bent pins. Replace module or DCC if persists. Maps to powermodule:not-detected.

What It Means: Power module "strike out" — faulted too many times and permanently disabled until power cycle. Module won't participate in charging.
Recommended Action: Power cycle to reset strike count. Replace module if recurs.

What It Means: AC input frequency outside 50/60Hz ±limits. Module can't sync.
Recommended Action: Check site power. If on generator, verify frequency stability.

What It Means: Ambient temp around power module above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Station may derate. Environmental.

What It Means: Rectifier (AC-DC) stage temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling. May derate.

What It Means: Charger (DC-DC) stage temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: AC phases connected in wrong order.
Recommended Action: Site electrician must swap two of the three phases at input.

What It Means: Temp sensor inside module has failed. Can't monitor own temperature.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Excessive humidity inside station. Risk of condensation on electronics.
Recommended Action: Check seals, cable glands, door gaskets. Inspect for water ingress.

What It Means: Conditions favorable for condensation.
Recommended Action: Monitor humidity. Check seals. Ensure ventilation.

What It Means: DC link input to charger stage below minimum.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Check rectifier stage. Power cycle.

What It Means: Internal transformer temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: Module processor watchdog triggered a reset. Transient firmware glitch.
Recommended Action: Monitor. If frequent, replace module.

What It Means: CPU1 executed illegal instruction. Firmware corruption or hardware fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: NMI triggered on CPU1. Critical hardware or firmware error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: CPU1 reset unexpectedly. May be watchdog or transient.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace module if frequent.

What It Means: Unhandled exception in CPU1 firmware. Software crash.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC bus discharge failed — voltage didn't bleed down after charging. Safety hazard — HV may persist.
Recommended Action: Do not open station. Wait and retry. Check discharge resistor. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Multiple temp sensors disagree. One may be drifting.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace module if readings diverge significantly.

What It Means: Module actively derating due to temperature. Self-protection.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Self-clears when temp drops.

What It Means: AC input voltage above maximum. Site voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Check site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: AC input voltage below minimum.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Check breakers and wiring.

What It Means: AC overcurrent — module drawing more than rated.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Check for short. Replace module if internal fault.

What It Means: DC output voltage exceeded maximum.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check regulation. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC output current exceeded maximum.
Recommended Action: Check for short in cable/contactor. Replace module if internal fault.

What It Means: DC link bus voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC link bus voltage too low. Rectifier not providing enough.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace module if rectifier failed.

What It Means: General charger (DC-DC) stage fault. Can't convert to required output.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Rectifier exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Charger stage exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Internal communication error within module (between CPUs or between CPU and FPGA).
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Output relay inside module has failed. Can't connect/disconnect from DC bus.
Recommended Action: Replace power module.

What It Means: Charger didn't reach target voltage during startup.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: DC output below target. Vehicle may not accept charge.
Recommended Action: Check module. Try different vehicle. Replace if regulation failed.

What It Means: Output channel A not ready. Failed startup check.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Output channel B not ready.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Internal switching frequency below expected. Control loop issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Voltage range switch failed. Can't change voltage range.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: DC link undervoltage caused shutdown.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Ambient temp exceeded max — module shut down.
Recommended Action: Environmental. Wait for temp to drop. Shade structure may be needed.

What It Means: Internal transformer exceeded max temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if transformer damaged.

What It Means: LLC converter primary overcurrent. Switching stage drawing excessive current.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: AC input current imbalance between phases.
Recommended Action: Check AC wiring. Check for failed rectifier leg. Replace module if internal.

What It Means: Voltage target sent to module is outside supported range. Software or config error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Module timed out during startup sequence. Didn't reach ready state in time.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: EPO activated. E-stop pressed or EPO circuit triggered.
Recommended Action: Check E-stop. Reset EPO. Investigate trigger cause.

What It Means: DC link voltage didn't reach setpoint. Precharge or rectifier underperforming.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge circuit. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Precharge circuit fault. Can't safely bring up DC link.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge components. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Module EEPROM error. Config/calibration data corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if permanent.

What It Means: FPGA/CPLD version doesn't match expected version for this firmware.
Recommended Action: Update firmware. Replace module if incompatible HW.

What It Means: Board ID pins or EEPROM don't match any known configuration.
Recommended Action: Check module HW. Replace if ID unresolvable.

What It Means: Internal 24V rail failed. Control circuits have no power.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Internal 5V rail failed. Digital logic has no power.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Internal 3.3V rail failed. Processor has no power.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Module lost CAN communication with station controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Module didn't reach ready state within expected time after boot.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Current sensor failed. Module can't measure own output current. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: PFC (Power Factor Correction) stage temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: LLC converter stage temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: Inter-processor communication (IPC) between CPU1 and CPU2 has failed. Dual-CPU module can't coordinate.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: IPC failure from CPU2 side.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: CPU2 executed illegal instruction. Firmware corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: NMI on CPU2. Critical HW or FW error.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: CPU2 reset unexpectedly. May be watchdog or transient.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace if frequent.

What It Means: Unhandled exception on CPU2. Software crash.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: CPU2 failed to boot. Secondary processor didn't start.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Module's internal fan showing signs of failure (speed mismatch or intermittent).
Recommended Action: Monitor. Schedule replacement.

What It Means: External flash memory SPI communication error. Firmware storage or logging may be affected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: PFC stage exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: LLC converter exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: CPU2 boot failure — secondary processor permanently failed to start.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: CPU2 RTOS task crashed. Real-time task failure on secondary processor.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Gate driver power supply fault. Switching transistor drivers have no power. Module can't switch.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: LLC converter enable check failed. Safety interlock preventing converter from starting.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: LLC output current sensor has failed. Module can't measure DC output current. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: FPGA SPI communication error. Logic device communication is unreliable.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: AC input voltage is near the high threshold. Not faulted yet but close to limit.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check site power.

What It Means: Module is actively switching voltage ranges. Informational — normal during wide voltage range transitions.
