Recommended Action: No action needed. Normal operation during range change.

What It Means: LLC converter control loop is at maximum duty cycle. Module is maxed out — can't provide more power.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check if vehicle demand exceeds module capability.

What It Means: Input relay (AC input contactor inside module) has failed. Module can't connect to AC supply.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: FPGA image update failed. Logic device firmware didn't program correctly.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Replace module if FPGA can't be reprogrammed.

What It Means: FPGA failed to boot. Logic device configuration is corrupted. Module inoperable.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Output relay failed to open. Relay may be welded. Module can't disconnect from DC bus. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Replace module. Inspect for relay welding.

What It Means: Output relay failed to close. Module can't connect to DC bus.
Recommended Action: Replace module or output relay.

What It Means: LLC converter calibration procedure failed. Output accuracy may be degraded.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Retry calibration. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Module physically installed but not detected on CAN bus.
Recommended Action: Check CAN connections. Reseat module. Check for bent pins. Replace module or controller if persists.

What It Means: Module failed to energize — couldn't bring up output voltage when commanded.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Precharge switch timed out. Precharge didn't complete in expected time.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge circuit. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Three-phase AC current sensor has failed. Module can't measure AC input current.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Precharge IC hardware has failed. Precharge control circuit is dead.
Recommended Action: Replace module.

What It Means: Power Controller lost communication with station controller.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace PWRCTL if persists.

What It Means: PWRCTL firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Cabinet fan 1 drawing overcurrent. Motor may be failing.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Cabinet fan 2 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan 1 open circuit — not powered. Wiring disconnected or motor coil broken.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Fan 2 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan 1 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor. Replace if degrading.

What It Means: Fan 2 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor.

What It Means: Fan 1 rotor is locked — motor jammed. Drawing current but not spinning.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstruction (debris, ice). Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Fan 2 rotor is locked.
Recommended Action: Check for obstruction. Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Ambient temperature RTD sensor is open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace sensor if failed.

What It Means: Ambient RTD sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or PWRCTL.

What It Means: Ambient temp above warning threshold. Hot environment.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Station may derate. Environmental.

What It Means: Ambient temp exceeded critical — station shutting down for thermal protection.
Recommended Action: Wait for temp to drop. Environmental. May need shade structure.

What It Means: PWRCTL board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: PWRCTL board exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace PWRCTL if persists.

What It Means: 48V input to PWRCTL is critically low — shut down. PWRCTL has no power.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS 48V output. Check cabling. Replace AuxPS or PWRCTL.

What It Means: 48V input below warning but still operational.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check AuxPS output.

What It Means: 48V input above warning. Regulation issue.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check AuxPS.

What It Means: 48V input critically high — PWRCTL shut down to protect components.
Recommended Action: Check AuxPS output. Replace AuxPS if voltage is out of regulation.

What It Means: Ethernet communication with PWRCTL lost. Network link down.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet cable. Power cycle. Usually self-recovers.

What It Means: Cabinet tilt exceeded safe threshold. Has been hit or is structurally compromised. Potential structural damage.
Recommended Action: Inspect immediately. Check mounting. Check for vehicle impact. Take offline if needed.

What It Means: Minor cabinet tilt detected. May have been bumped.
Recommended Action: Inspect mounting. Monitor.

What It Means: DC section thermal switch has an error — switch itself may have failed or wiring is broken.
Recommended Action: Check thermal switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: AC section thermal switch error.
Recommended Action: Check thermal switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: DC Bus A thermal switch tripped — station derating on Bus A. Charging at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Check Bus A connections. Fix root cause of overheating.

What It Means: DC Bus B thermal switch tripped — derating.
Recommended Action: Check Bus B connections.

What It Means: DC Bus C thermal switch tripped — derating.
Recommended Action: Check Bus C connections.

What It Means: DC Bus D thermal switch tripped — derating.
Recommended Action: Check Bus D connections.

What It Means: DC Bus A thermal switch error — switch failed or wiring broken.
Recommended Action: Check switch. Replace if failed.

What It Means: DC Bus B thermal switch error.
Recommended Action: Check switch. Replace.

What It Means: DC Bus C thermal switch error.
Recommended Action: Check switch. Replace.

What It Means: DC Bus D thermal switch error.
Recommended Action: Check switch. Replace.

What It Means: Power cabinet door 1 is open. Tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door. Check latch.

What It Means: Power cabinet door 2 is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door.

What It Means: Power cabinet door 3 is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door.

What It Means: HV surge arrestor in power cabinet has tripped. HV surge protection depleted — station vulnerable to surges.
Recommended Action: Replace HV surge arrestor immediately. Check for surge damage.

What It Means: AC surge arrestor in power cabinet has tripped. AC surge protection depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace AC surge arrestor. Check site power quality.

What It Means: PWRCTL data partition full. Logs, sessions, firmware updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Clear logs. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: PWRCTL temp partition full. Temp file operations and updates will fail.
Recommended Action: Reboot to clear temp files.

What It Means: Cooling Controller lost communication with station controller. Cooling status unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace COOLCTRL if persists.

What It Means: COOLCTRL firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Fan 1 drawing overcurrent — motor may be failing or obstructed.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Fan 2 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan 3 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 3.

What It Means: Fan 4 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Replace fan 4.

What It Means: Fan 1 has open circuit — wiring disconnected or motor coil broken. Not powered.
Recommended Action: Check fan wiring. Replace fan 1.

What It Means: Fan 2 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 2.

What It Means: Fan 3 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 3.

What It Means: Fan 4 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Replace fan 4.

What It Means: Fan 1 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor. Replace if degrading.

What It Means: Fan 2 RPM mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor.

What It Means: Fan 3 RPM mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor.

What It Means: Fan 4 RPM mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Monitor.

What It Means: Coolant pump 1 drawing overcurrent — motor failing, impeller jammed, or fluid blockage.
Recommended Action: Check for blockage in coolant lines. Replace pump 1 if persists.

What It Means: Coolant pump 2 drawing overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check for blockage. Replace pump 2 if persists.

What It Means: Pump 1 open circuit — not powered. Wiring disconnected or motor dead.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Replace pump 1.

What It Means: Pump 2 open circuit.
Recommended Action: Check pump wiring. Replace pump 2.

What It Means: Pump 1 RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
Recommended Action: Check pump. Monitor. Replace if degrading.

What It Means: Pump 2 RPM mismatch.
Recommended Action: Check pump. Monitor.

What It Means: Coolant level critically low. Risk of pump dry-run and overheating.
Recommended Action: Top up coolant immediately. Check for leaks. Replace pump if damaged.

What It Means: Coolant at medium-low level. Not critical but trending down.
Recommended Action: Schedule coolant top-up. Check for slow leaks.

What It Means: Coolant level sensor is open/disconnected. Can't monitor coolant level — safety risk.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection. Replace sensor if failed.

What It Means: COOLCTRL board temp above warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check ventilation.

What It Means: COOLCTRL board exceeded critical temp — shut down.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace COOLCTRL if persists.

What It Means: NTC coolant temp sensor 0 open/disconnected. Reading unavailable.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: NTC coolant temp sensor 1 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: NTC coolant temp sensor 2 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: NTC coolant temp sensor 3 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 0 shorted — false high-temp readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 2 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 3 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 0 reading inconsistent with expected values.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace if unreliable.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 1 mismatch.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace if unreliable.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 2 mismatch.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace if unreliable.

What It Means: NTC coolant sensor 3 mismatch.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace if unreliable.

What It Means: Coolant temperature above warning. Cooling system may be underperforming.
Recommended Action: Check fans, pump, coolant level. Check for airflow obstructions.

What It Means: Coolant temperature exceeded critical — station shutting down for thermal protection.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system immediately. Top up coolant. Check fans and pump.

What It Means: Ambient temperature NTC sensor open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: Ambient NTC sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: PCB temperature NTC sensor open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: PCB NTC sensor shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor or COOLCTRL.

What It Means: Coolant flow sensor 1 stuck in inactive state. Not detecting any flow even though pump may be running.
Recommended Action: Check sensor wiring. Check for blockage. Replace sensor if persists.

What It Means: Coolant flow sensor 2 stuck inactive.
Recommended Action: Check sensor. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Coolant flow sensor 1 stuck in active state. Reporting flow even when pump is off — sensor is faulty.
Recommended Action: Replace flow sensor 1.

What It Means: Coolant flow sensor 2 stuck active.
Recommended Action: Replace flow sensor 2.

What It Means: Firmware error — pump states don't match expected values. Software thinks pumps are in a different state than hardware reports.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace COOLCTRL if persists.

What It Means: Coolant flow is restricted — partial blockage in the cooling loop. Flow rate below expected threshold.
Recommended Action: Check for kinks in coolant hoses. Check for debris in filter. Flush system if needed.

What It Means: SEVB detected invalid or unsupported cable. EEPROM data doesn't match expected cable type.
Recommended Action: Verify correct cable installed. Check cable EEPROM. Replace cable if wrong.

What It Means: SEVB configuration is invalid — software or hardware config mismatch.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure SEVB. Update firmware. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: SEVB lost communication with station controller (Falcon/DCC). Entire dispenser is offline.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet/CAN cable to SEVB. Power cycle. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: SEVB lost communication with LCC (Liquid-Cooled Cable controller). Cable temp monitoring offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable between SEVB and LCC. Power cycle. Replace LCC or SEVB if persists.

What It Means: PLC (Power Line Communication) on SEVB has failed. CCS vehicle communication impossible.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: PowerLink cable temperature exceeded critical threshold. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Check cooling. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Panther (CP6000) cable temperature exceeded critical. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Check cooling. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: SEVB board itself is overheating.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation in dispenser. Power cycle. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: SEVB LED indicator drawing overcurrent. LED driver or strip may be short-circuiting.
Recommended Action: Check LED wiring. Replace LED module. Non-critical.

What It Means: Vehicle power supply from SEVB has failed. SEVB can't provide pilot/communication power to vehicle connector.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check SEVB power supply circuit. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: PowerLink cable not detected by SEVB. EEPROM or physical connection failure.
Recommended Action: Check cable seating. Check EEPROM. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Panther cable not detected by SEVB.
Recommended Action: Check cable seating. Check EEPROM. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: PowerLink cable temp sensor 0 open/disconnected.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: PowerLink cable temp sensor 1 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: PowerLink cable temp sensor 0 shorted — false readings.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: PowerLink cable temp sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: Panther cable temp sensor 0 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: Panther cable temp sensor 1 open.
Recommended Action: Check sensor connection.

What It Means: Panther cable temp sensor 0 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: Panther cable temp sensor 1 shorted.
Recommended Action: Replace cable.

What It Means: Cable cut detected by SEVB. Physical cable damage or disconnection. Lockout fault.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for vandalism/damage. Replace cable. Clear lockout via installer app.

What It Means: Temperature sensor on SEVB or cable has failed completely.
Recommended Action: Check sensors. Replace SEVB or cable if sensor is integrated.

What It Means: SEVB EEPROM has failed. Cable identity and configuration data lost.
Recommended Action: Replace SEVB or cable.

What It Means: EV diode check failed — vehicle's diode appears shorted. Abnormal pilot voltage detected.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists with all vehicles, replace SEVB.

What It Means: Cable ampacity configuration is invalid — max current rating doesn't match physical cable.
Recommended Action: Verify cable rating. Check EEPROM. Replace cable or reconfigure.

What It Means: Safety CPU register check failed. Processor registers corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety non-stack register check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety floating-point register 1 check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety floating-point register 2 check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety PRIMASK register check failed — interrupt masking corrupt.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety special register check failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety program counter register failed — firmware execution integrity compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety stack check failed — stack corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety RAM check failed — memory corruption.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety NVM check failed — stored data corrupted.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety clock check failed — timing integrity compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety MPU fault — unauthorized memory access.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Safety strap configuration invalid — hardware config pins wrong.
Recommended Action: Check SEVB hardware. Replace SEVB.

What It Means: Coolant temperature at SEVB/cable exceeded critical — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check coolant flow. Check pump. Top up coolant. Replace cable or SEVB if persists.

What It Means: Temperature data from LCC is invalid — out of expected range.
Recommended Action: Check LCC sensor connections. Replace LCC if persists.

What It Means: Coolant flow through the cable is restricted — partial blockage.
Recommended Action: Check for kinks in coolant hoses. Check filter. Flush system if needed.

What It Means: Safety Controller lost communication with station controller. Safety monitoring is offline. Critical safety concern.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace SAFETYCTRL if persists. Station should not charge without safety controller.

What It Means: Safety Controller firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: 48V supply from AuxPS to Safety Controller is below warning threshold.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check AuxPS output.

What It Means: Internal 5V rail on Safety Controller is below warning.
Recommended Action: Monitor. If drops further, replace SAFETYCTRL.

What It Means: Internal 3.3V rail below warning. Processor and comms circuits may become unreliable.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Replace SAFETYCTRL if voltage drops further.

What It Means: Safety line configuration 1 is invalid. The safety interlock chain configuration doesn't match expected settings. Station may not be safe to operate.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure safety line. Verify hardware wiring matches config. Replace SAFETYCTRL if config can't be resolved. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: Safety line configuration 2 is invalid. Safety interlock chain config doesn't match expected settings. Station may not be safe to operate.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure safety line. Verify HW wiring matches config. Replace SAFETYCTRL if unresolvable. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: Safety line configuration 3 is invalid.
Recommended Action: Same as above.

What It Means: Safety line configuration 4 is invalid.
Recommended Action: Same as above.

What It Means: Safety line feedback 1 is invalid. The physical safety interlock chain feedback signal doesn't match expected state. A safety relay or contactor may have failed.
Recommended Action: Check safety relay 1. Verify wiring. Replace failed component. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: Safety line feedback 2 is invalid.
Recommended Action: Check safety relay 2. Verify wiring. Replace failed component.

What It Means: Safety line feedback 3 is invalid.
Recommended Action: Check safety relay 3. Verify wiring. Replace failed component.

What It Means: Safety line feedback 4 is invalid.
Recommended Action: Check safety relay 4. Verify wiring. Replace failed component.

What It Means: Smart antenna module lost communication with station controller. Cellular connectivity may be degraded.
Recommended Action: Check antenna cable connections. Power cycle. Replace antenna module if persists.

What It Means: Smart antenna firmware update failed.
Recommended Action: Retry update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: AC input phase is incorrect or abnormal. One phase may be missing or voltage is wrong.
Recommended Action: Check AC input wiring. Verify all phases. Contact site electrician.

What It Means: Station tilt exceeded safe threshold. Station has been hit by a vehicle or is structurally compromised.
Recommended Action: Inspect immediately. Check mounting bolts. Check for vehicle impact. Take offline if needed.

What It Means: Impact/shock sensor triggered. Station was hit or experienced significant vibration.
Recommended Action: Inspect station for physical damage. Check mounting. Check all connectors for loosening.

What It Means: Internal communication error between station subsystems. Generic CAN/serial failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all internal cables. Replace DCC or Falcon if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr fan 0 is stuck/not spinning. No cooling for SmartPwr board.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan. Maps to smartpwr:fan0-stuck.

What It Means: SmartPwr fan 1 is stuck/not spinning.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Replace fan. Maps to smartpwr:fan1-stuck.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 0 output voltage below minimum. Subsystem on this channel may not function.
Recommended Action: Check load on channel 0. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 1 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load on channel 1. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 2 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 3 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 4 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 5 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 6 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 7 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 0 output voltage above maximum. Regulation failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 1 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 2 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 3 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 4 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 5 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 6 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 7 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 0 overcurrent. Subsystem drawing too much. Channel may shut down.
Recommended Action: Check connected subsystem for short. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 1 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 2 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 3 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 4 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 5 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 6 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: SmartPwr channel 7 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: AC input to SmartPwr is over voltage. Site power too high.
Recommended Action: Check site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: SmartPwr is in test mode. Should not be in test mode during normal operation. Informational.
Recommended Action: Exit test mode. Power cycle if stuck.

What It Means: SmartPwr PLL (Phase-Locked Loop) is locked. Usually informational — indicates SmartPwr is synced to AC input frequency. Can be a fault if PLL locks to wrong frequency.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Usually normal. If accompanied by other faults, investigate.

What It Means: General SmartPwr power fault. Internal power distribution has failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr lost CAN communication with station controller. Power distribution status unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr CAN bus is in non-operational state. Bus error recovery failed. Communication permanently broken until reset.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check CAN bus wiring and termination (120Ω). Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: SmartPwr fan 0 not spinning. No cooling for SmartPwr board.
Recommended Action: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: SmartPwr fan 1 not spinning.
Recommended Action: Check fan. Replace fan.

What It Means: Channel 0 output below minimum voltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Channel 1 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 2 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 3 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 4 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 6 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 7 undervoltage.
Recommended Action: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 0 output above maximum. Regulation failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Channel 1 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 2 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 3 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 4 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 6 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 7 overvoltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 0 overcurrent. Subsystem drawing too much. Channel may shut down.
Recommended Action: Check connected subsystem for short. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 1 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 2 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 3 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 4 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 6 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Channel 7 overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: AC input to SmartPwr over voltage.
Recommended Action: Check site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: SmartPwr in test mode during normal operation.
Recommended Action: Exit test mode. Power cycle if stuck.

What It Means: PLL locked status. Usually informational — synced to AC frequency.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Usually normal.

What It Means: General SmartPwr power fault. Internal power distribution failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Panel A is open. Tamper/door switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel A. Check latch.

What It Means: Panel B is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel B.

What It Means: Panel C is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel C.

What It Means: Panel D is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel D.

What It Means: Panel E is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel E.

What It Means: Panel F is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel F.

What It Means: Panel G is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel G.

What It Means: Panel H is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel H.

What It Means: Panel I is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel I.

What It Means: Panel J is open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel J.

What It Means: Flood sensor triggered — water detected inside station. Electrical hazard.
Recommended Action: Stop station immediately. Inspect for water ingress. Check seals, cable glands, drainage. Dry out station before restarting.

What It Means: External emergency stop triggered. An external E-stop button (wall-mounted or pole-mounted) was pressed.
Recommended Action: Check external E-stop. Reset E-stop. Investigate why triggered.

What It Means: AC surge protector has tripped. AC surge protection depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace AC surge protector. Check site power quality.

What It Means: AC surge protector is missing or not detected. Station has no AC surge protection.
Recommended Action: Install/replace AC surge protector.

What It Means: AC input terminal 1 is overheating. Loose connection or undersized wire. Fire hazard.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque. Inspect for discoloration. Re-torque or replace.

What It Means: AC input terminal 2 is overheating.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque.

What It Means: AC input terminal 3 is overheating.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque.

What It Means: Front bottom panel of dispenser is open. Tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel. Check latch.

What It Means: Middle vent panel is open. Ventilation opening exposed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Front top panel open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Secondary display panel open. Display area exposed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Light bar panel open. LED light bar area exposed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Rear bottom panel open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Rear middle panel open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Rear top panel open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Left extrusion panel open. Side access panel removed.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Right extrusion panel open.
Recommended Action: Close and secure panel.

What It Means: Flood sensor triggered — water detected inside dispenser. Electrical hazard.
Recommended Action: Stop station. Inspect for water ingress. Check seals and drainage. Dry out before restarting.

What It Means: External emergency stop triggered on dispenser.
Recommended Action: Check E-stop. Reset. Investigate trigger.

What It Means: AC surge protector 1 in dispenser has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace surge protector 1.

What It Means: AC surge protector 2 has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace surge protector 2.

What It Means: AC surge protector missing or not detected in dispenser.
Recommended Action: Install/replace surge protector.

What It Means: AC input temperature inside dispenser is too hot. Fire hazard.
Recommended Action: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque. Inspect for loose connections.

What It Means: DISPIO lost CAN communication with dispenser controller. Dispenser I/O monitoring is offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace DISPIO if persists.

What It Means: Station derating output power due to high internal temperature. Self-protecting by limiting current.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Check ambient temp. Ensure airflow unobstructed.

What It Means: A power module inside the power block has failed. Station may charge at reduced power on remaining modules.
Recommended Action: Identify faulted module. Power cycle. Replace failed module.

What It Means: Power interconnect migration between power blocks failed. In multi-block configurations, power couldn't be transferred.
Recommended Action: Check interconnect cabling. Power cycle. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Power interconnect is in unsafe state. Voltage mismatch or isolation failure between connected power blocks. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Check interconnect. Power cycle. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Power block safety check failed. One or more safety systems didn't pass self-test.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check safety circuits. Escalate to engineering. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: Tamper detected on power block. Door/panel opened or tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure enclosure. Check tamper switch.

What It Means: Power block is locked out. A critical fault has triggered a lockout — station won't charge until manually cleared.
Recommended Action: Identify root cause. Fix underlying fault. Clear lockout via installer app or console.

What It Means: A peer power block (in multi-block config) is locked out. This block may be affected.
Recommended Action: Fix peer block's lockout first.

What It Means: A peer power block is in an unsafe state. This block disconnects from peer for safety.
Recommended Action: Fix peer block faults first.

What It Means: Required devices (power modules, meters, contactors) not detected in chassis. Chassis can't operate.
Recommended Action: Check device seating. Check CAN bus. Power cycle. Replace missing/failed devices.

What It Means: Entire power block cluster is unsafe. All interconnected blocks have a safety concern.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Power cycle all blocks. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Power block lost communication with station controller (MCB).
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet/CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace power block controller if persists.

What It Means: Short circuit detected on power block output. DC output is shorted. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Inspect DC cables and contactors. Check for welded relays. Replace damaged components.

What It Means: Power block failed to energize for charging. Couldn't bring up DC output voltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules and contactors. Replace failed components.

What It Means: One or more AC input phases missing at power block.
Recommended Action: Check breakers. Verify all phases present. Contact site electrician.

What It Means: Charge meter has failed. Energy measurement offline. Billing accuracy affected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace meter module if persists.

What It Means: One or more AC input phases missing.
Recommended Action: Check breakers. Verify all phases. Contact site electrician.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during cable safety check (pre-charge). Cable or station insulation is compromised. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for damage. Megger test. Replace cable if insulation failed.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during active charging. Insulation resistance dropped below minimum. Safety hazard — charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect cables and contactors. Megger test. Replace damaged components.

What It Means: Overcurrent detected during charging. Current exceeded safe limits.
Recommended Action: Check for short circuit. Power cycle. Replace contactors or cables if damaged.

What It Means: Output voltage exceeded maximum during charging.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module regulation. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Negative voltage detected on DC output. Polarity reversal or control failure. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Power cycle. Check contactors and power modules. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Proximity pilot signal not detected. Cable may not be connected or PP circuit is open.
Recommended Action: Check cable connection. Check PP circuit. Replace cable if PP wire is broken.

What It Means: PowerLink proximity pilot is open. Cable PP resistor missing or wire broken. Station can't detect cable current rating.
Recommended Action: Check PP circuit in connector. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Proximity pilot signal was present but lost during session. Cable may have been partially unplugged.
Recommended Action: Check cable connection. Check connector latch. Try reconnecting.

What It Means: Control pilot signal lost during session. Vehicle communication interrupted.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit. Try different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Control pilot voltage is invalid — not matching any valid J1772/CCS state.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit wiring. Try different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Control pilot signal is shorted to ground. Pilot circuit is damaged. Station will not charge.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Inspect connector for damage. Replace cable or cable controller.

What It Means: Cable connector not fully inserted into vehicle inlet.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector. Check connector latch.

What It Means: Tamper detected during charging. Door opened or tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure enclosure. Check tamper switch.

What It Means: A power module has failed during or before a charging session.
Recommended Action: Identify faulted module. Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Station derating due to high temperature. Charging continues at reduced power.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Check ambient temp.

What It Means: Internal power supply voltage has deviated from expected value.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AuxPS. Replace if voltage unstable.

What It Means: Internal power supply current has deviated. Load imbalance or supply degradation.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AuxPS. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Electrical surge detected. Surge protection may have triggered.
Recommended Action: Check surge protectors. Replace if triggered. Check site power quality.

What It Means: Entire power system has failed. Station cannot produce DC power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AC input. Check power modules. Replace as needed.

What It Means: Vehicle reported voltage outside station's supported range.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module voltage range.

What It Means: Vehicle forced an emergency stop. The EV's BMS triggered an emergency shutdown during charging.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If recurring, check station output for voltage/current anomalies.

What It Means: Cable insulation check detected short circuit. DC cables or connector damaged. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable and connector. Megger test. Replace cable.

What It Means: Cable insulation check error — test couldn't complete. Safety can't be verified.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon module. Replace cable or IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: All charging cables on the station are overheating. No cable available for safe charging. Station goes offline.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system for all cables. Check coolant flow. Replace cables if damaged.

What It Means: Insulation Monitoring Device functional check failed. IMD can't verify its own operation. Safety system compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Unit is locked out due to critical fault. Station won't charge until lockout is manually cleared.
Recommended Action: Identify root cause. Fix underlying fault. Clear lockout via installer app or console.

What It Means: Safety check failed. One or more safety systems didn't pass. Station should not charge.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all safety circuits. Escalate to engineering. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: Required devices (power modules, meters, contactors) not detected in chassis. Station can't operate.
Recommended Action: Check device seating. Check CAN bus. Power cycle. Replace missing/failed devices.

What It Means: Short circuit on DC output. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Inspect DC cables and contactors. Check for welded relays. Replace damaged components.

What It Means: A peer unit (in multi-unit config) is in unsafe state. This unit disconnects from peer.
Recommended Action: Fix peer unit faults first.

What It Means: Cable-to-port mapping failed. Station can't determine which cable belongs to which charging port.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure cable mapping. Check EEPROM data. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Lion lost communication with station controller. Charging session management offline.
Recommended Action: Check Ethernet/CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace controller if persists.

What It Means: Power system locked out due to repeated critical faults. Won't charge until manually cleared.
Recommended Action: Identify root cause. Fix underlying fault. Clear lockout via installer app or console.

What It Means: Power system is completely inoperable. No power modules available.
Recommended Action: Check all power modules. Power cycle. Replace failed modules.

What It Means: Failed to reserve power modules for a charging session. All modules may be in use or faulted.
Recommended Action: Check module status. Power cycle. Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: Failed to set charging parameters on power system. Voltage/current targets couldn't be configured.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Power system failed to energize — couldn't bring up DC output voltage.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules and contactors. Replace failed components.

What It Means: Power system failed to de-energize — DC voltage didn't ramp down properly. Safety hazard — HV may persist.
Recommended Action: Do not open station. Wait and retry. Check discharge circuit. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Power system couldn't meet the current demand. Modules not delivering requested current.
Recommended Action: Check module status. Some modules may be derated or faulted. Replace as needed.

What It Means: Power module reservation was lost during session. Module became unavailable mid-charge.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check module that dropped out. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Communication with power system lost during session. Can't control power delivery.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/Ethernet cabling. Power cycle. Replace controller if persists.

What It Means: Power module migration between ports failed. In dynamic power sharing, modules couldn't be reassigned.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check interconnect. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Power interconnect configuration is invalid. Wiring or config doesn't match expected topology.
Recommended Action: Verify interconnect wiring. Reconfigure. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Power interconnect is unsafe — voltage mismatch or isolation failure between connected units. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Check interconnect. Power cycle. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Entire cluster of interconnected units is unsafe.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Power cycle all units. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Failed to energize for charging. Couldn't bring up DC output.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power modules and contactors.

What It Means: Cable cut detected. Physical cable damage. Lockout fault.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable for vandalism/damage. Replace cable. Clear lockout via installer app.

What It Means: Energy meter is offline. Billing accuracy affected and energy measurement unavailable.
Recommended Action: Check meter CAN connection. Power cycle. Replace meter if persists.

What It Means: General overcurrent detected. Station drawing more than allowed.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: AC-specific overcurrent through charging circuit.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and breaker. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Soft AC overcurrent — current slightly above limit. Station may reduce current rather than fault.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check wiring. If recurring, investigate load.

What It Means: Station can't provide required ventilation. Some vehicles (rare, older models) require ventilated charging — station can't comply.
Recommended Action: Informational for most modern EVs. Check if specific vehicle requires ventilation.

What It Means: Recoverable ground fault — may clear after EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for session end. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Hard GFCI trip — significant ground fault detected.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: AC Controller self-test failed. Hardware diagnostic didn't pass.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Replace MCC if doesn't recover.

What It Means: AC hardware error — generic hardware failure in AC charging circuit.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Communication with AC Controller (MCC) lost. Can't manage charging.
Recommended Action: Check CAN/SPI cabling. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Vehicle diode check failed — vehicle's pilot diode appears faulty.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists with all vehicles, check station pilot circuit.

What It Means: Control pilot signal shorted to ground. Pilot circuit damaged.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Inspect connector. Replace cable or MCC.

What It Means: Cable connector not fully inserted into vehicle.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector. Check latch.

What It Means: Station temperature exceeded critical threshold. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation and ambient temp. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Analytics system detected an intelligent pin status violation — a connector pin is not in the expected electrical state. May indicate connector damage or corrosion.
Recommended Action: Inspect connector pins. Check for corrosion, bent pins, or debris. Replace connector if damaged.

What It Means: Entire power system failed. Station can't produce DC power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AC input, power modules, contactors. Replace as needed.

What It Means: AC overcurrent detected at platform level.
Recommended Action: Check wiring. Check ACB. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: Soft AC overcurrent — slightly above limit. Station may reduce current.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check wiring. If recurring, investigate load.

What It Means: Recoverable ground fault at platform level. May clear after EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for session end. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Non-recoverable ground fault. Occurs as relay closes (NA). Auto-clears when EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for EV disconnect. If recurring, replace ACB.

What It Means: Vehicle's control pilot diode appears faulty. Station can't verify vehicle-side diode.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists with all vehicles, check station pilot circuit.

What It Means: Control pilot shorted to ground at platform level.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Inspect connector. Replace cable controller or ACB.

What It Means: Station temperature exceeded critical threshold. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Check ambient temp. Power cycle.

What It Means: ACB self-test failed at platform level.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Replace ACB if doesn't recover.

What It Means: ACB firmware failed at platform level.
Recommended Action: Update firmware via NOS. Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: AC hardware error at platform level. Generic ACB hardware failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace ACB if persists.

What It Means: General cable error — cable has a fault that doesn't fit other specific categories.
Recommended Action: Check cable. Check cable controller. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: SEVB safety system failure. Cable controller's safety checks failed. Station should not charge.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: EV equipment error — vehicle-side equipment (connector, inlet) has an issue. Station detected abnormal vehicle behavior.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists with all vehicles, check station.

What It Means: Tamper detected at platform level. Door/panel opened or impact detected.
Recommended Action: Close and secure enclosure. Check tamper switches.

What It Means: ACB communication lost at platform level.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cable. Power cycle. Replace ACB or Falcon if persists.

What It Means: Eichrecht-certified meter reading doesn't match station's internal measurement. Billing integrity compromised. Legally required in EU.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check meter calibration. Replace meter if persists. Escalate — legal compliance issue.

What It Means: Digital signature verification failed on Eichrecht data. Data integrity can't be verified. Legal compliance issue in EU.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check firmware. Escalate — legal compliance issue.

What It Means: AC input voltage is out of acceptable range at platform level.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Verify breaker and wiring.

What It Means: Station can't provide required ventilation for vehicles that request it.
Recommended Action: Informational for most modern EVs. Non-critical.

What It Means: Cable cut detected at platform level. Lockout fault.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Replace cable. Clear lockout.

What It Means: ZLB (Zählerplatz/meter housing) adapter lost. Eichrecht meter adapter communication failed. EU-specific.
Recommended Action: Check ZLB adapter connection. Replace adapter if persists.

What It Means: Cable not fully inserted into vehicle at platform level.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector. Check latch.

What It Means: Grid undervoltage detected — station suspended charging. Voltage dropped below minimum threshold.
Recommended Action: Check site power. Station will auto-resume when voltage recovers. If persistent, contact utility.

What It Means: Grid voltage is unstable — fluctuating outside normal range. Station may suspend or derate.
Recommended Action: Check site power quality. May indicate utility-side issue.

What It Means: Grid frequency is unstable — not maintaining stable 50/60Hz. Station may suspend.
Recommended Action: Check site power. If on generator, verify governor stability.

What It Means: General overcurrent detected.
Recommended Action: Check breaker size and wiring. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: AC-specific overcurrent.
Recommended Action: Check wiring and breaker. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Soft AC overcurrent — slightly above limit. May reduce current.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check wiring.

What It Means: Can't provide required ventilation for vehicles that request it.
Recommended Action: Informational for most EVs. Non-critical.

What It Means: Recoverable ground fault. May clear after EV disconnects.
Recommended Action: Wait for session end. If recurring, investigate wiring.

What It Means: Hard GFCI trip — significant ground fault.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect wiring. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: MCC self-test failed. Hardware diagnostic didn't pass.
Recommended Action: Power cycle a few times. Replace MCC if doesn't recover.

What It Means: AC hardware error — generic hardware failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Communication with MCC lost.
Recommended Action: Check cabling. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Vehicle pilot diode appears faulty.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. If persists with all, check station.

What It Means: Tamper detected — door/panel opened or impact.
Recommended Action: Close and secure enclosure. Check tamper switch.

What It Means: Control pilot shorted to ground.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Replace cable or MCC.

What It Means: Cable not fully inserted into vehicle.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector.

What It Means: Station exceeded critical temp. Charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check ventilation. Power cycle. Replace MCC if persists.

What It Means: Entire power system failed. No DC power available.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check AC input, modules, contactors.

What It Means: Vehicle forced emergency stop. EV's BMS triggered emergency shutdown.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check station output for anomalies.

What It Means: Vehicle's control pilot diode appears faulty.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Cable insulation check detected short circuit. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Megger test. Replace cable.

What It Means: Cable insulation check error — test couldn't complete.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon. Replace cable or IsoMon.

What It Means: IMD functional check failed. Safety system compromised.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Station thermal shutdown — internal temp exceeded maximum safe limit. All charging stopped.
Recommended Action: Check entire cooling system. Wait for cool-down. Power cycle.

What It Means: Ambient temperature reading is invalid — sensor returning out-of-range values. Station can't make thermal decisions.
Recommended Action: Check ambient temp sensor. Replace sensor.

What It Means: Cable is already too hot before charging starts. Can't begin session.
Recommended Action: Wait for cable to cool. Check cooling system. Replace cable if damaged.

What It Means: Cable RTD sensors disagree during pre-check. Sensor reliability questionable.
Recommended Action: Check cable sensors. Replace cable if sensors are failing.

What It Means: Temperature sensor has failed. Can't monitor temp at that location.
Recommended Action: Replace sensor.

What It Means: PP signal not detected. Cable may not be connected.
Recommended Action: Check cable connection. Check PP circuit.

What It Means: PP is open. PP resistor missing or wire broken.
Recommended Action: Check PP circuit in connector. Replace cable.

What It Means: PP signal lost during session. Cable may have been partially unplugged.
Recommended Action: Check cable connection. Check connector latch.

What It Means: Control pilot not detected. No vehicle communication.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit. Check cable controller.

What It Means: Control pilot lost during session.
Recommended Action: Check pilot circuit. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Control pilot voltage invalid — not matching valid CCS state.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Control pilot shorted to ground. Pilot circuit damaged.
Recommended Action: Check pilot wiring. Replace cable or cable controller.

What It Means: Cable not fully inserted.
Recommended Action: Instruct driver to fully insert connector.

What It Means: Power interconnect unsafe — voltage mismatch or isolation failure. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Check interconnect. Escalate.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during cable safety check. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Megger test. Replace cable.

What It Means: DC isolation lost during active charging. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Inspect cables and contactors.

What It Means: Platform-level safety check failed. Do not charge.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all safety circuits. Escalate.

What It Means: A component has a non-critical issue that may degrade performance but doesn't prevent charging.
Recommended Action: Identify component. Schedule maintenance.

What It Means: UL (Underwriters Laboratories) safety stop triggered. A UL-certified safety condition was violated. Regulatory compliance issue.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Investigate root cause. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: SEVB safety system failure at platform level.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace SEVB. Do not charge until resolved.

What It Means: EV equipment error — vehicle-side issue detected.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle.

What It Means: Cable cut detected. Lockout fault.
Recommended Action: Inspect cable. Replace cable. Clear lockout.

What It Means: Tamper detected at platform level.
Recommended Action: Close and secure enclosure.

What It Means: A surge arrestor has tripped. Surge protection depleted.
Recommended Action: Replace surge arrestor. Check site power quality.

What It Means: Unit locked out due to critical fault. Won't charge until manually cleared.
Recommended Action: Fix underlying fault. Clear lockout via installer app.

What It Means: Peer unit locked out. May affect this unit.
Recommended Action: Fix peer unit's lockout first.

What It Means: Peer unit is unsafe. This unit disconnects from peer.
Recommended Action: Fix peer unit faults.

What It Means: Required devices not detected in chassis.
Recommended Action: Check device seating. Check CAN bus. Power cycle.

What It Means: Vehicle reported voltage outside supported range.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module range.

What It Means: Vehicle precharge voltage is invalid — vehicle's battery voltage doesn't match expected range during precharge phase.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module configuration.

What It Means: Overcurrent during charging.
Recommended Action: Check for short circuit. Power cycle. Replace contactors/cables if damaged.

What It Means: Output voltage exceeded maximum.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check power module regulation.

What It Means: Negative voltage detected on DC output. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Power cycle. Check contactors and modules. Escalate.

What It Means: Cable locking holsters are inoperable. Cables can't be locked in holster position.
Recommended Action: Check holster lock motor. Replace holster mechanism if persists.

What It Means: DC hardware error — generic DC-side hardware failure.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace faulted DC component.

What It Means: Fan current reading error. Can't measure fan current draw accurately.
Recommended Action: Check fan current sensor. Replace cooling controller if persists.

What It Means: Entire cluster is unsafe. All interconnected units have safety concern.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Power cycle all units. Escalate.

What It Means: Excessive capacitance detected on DC output. Vehicle's input capacitance exceeds station's safe limit.
Recommended Action: Try different vehicle. Check for cable damage that could add capacitance.

What It Means: DC input contactor is welded — contacts fused. Can't open. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Replace welded contactor. Inspect for arc damage.

What It Means: Communication with MDS (Main Distribution Switch) lost. Contactor status unknown.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to MDS. Power cycle. Replace MDS if persists.

What It Means: Eichrecht meter reading mismatch. Billing integrity compromised. EU legal compliance issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check meter. Escalate — legal compliance.

What It Means: Digital signature verification failed on Eichrecht data. EU legal compliance issue.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check firmware. Escalate.

What It Means: Power system locked out.
Recommended Action: Fix root cause. Clear lockout.

What It Means: Power system completely inoperable.
Recommended Action: Check all modules. Power cycle. Replace failed modules.

What It Means: Failed to reserve power modules for session.
Recommended Action: Check module status. Power cycle.

What It Means: Failed to set charging parameters.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Update firmware.

What It Means: Failed to energize DC output.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check modules and contactors.

What It Means: Failed to de-energize. HV may persist.
Recommended Action: Do not open station. Wait and retry. Check discharge circuit.

What It Means: Couldn't meet current demand.
Recommended Action: Check module status. Replace derated/faulted modules.

What It Means: Module reservation lost during session.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check module that dropped.

What It Means: Communication with power system lost.
Recommended Action: Check cabling. Power cycle.

What It Means: Power module migration between ports failed.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check interconnect. Escalate.

What It Means: Power interconnect configuration invalid.
Recommended Action: Verify wiring. Reconfigure. Escalate.

What It Means: ZLB (Eichrecht meter housing) adapter communication lost. EU-specific.
Recommended Action: Check ZLB adapter connection. Replace if persists.

What It Means: All AC input relays stuck open. Station can't receive AC power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace relays.

What It Means: Input relay cluster is in unsafe state. Multiple relays have faults.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Inspect all input relays. Replace as needed.

What It Means: Output relay cluster is in unsafe state. Multiple output contactors have faults.
Recommended Action: Do not charge. Inspect all output contactors. Replace as needed.

What It Means: Energy meter offline. Billing and measurement unavailable.
Recommended Action: Check meter CAN connection. Power cycle. Replace meter.

What It Means: Combined output from parallel modules has an error. Voltage/current mismatch.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check all module outputs. Replace faulted module.

What It Means: Station configuration is invalid. Software or hardware config mismatch.
Recommended Action: Reconfigure station. Update firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Fault code 24 — typically maps to a power module communication error or CAN bus issue with a specific module.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Identify which module is affected.

What It Means: Fault code 27 — typically maps to power module over-temperature warning. Module is running hot.
Recommended Action: Check cooling system. Module may be derating.

What It Means: Fault code 29 — typically maps to power module under-voltage. DC link or output voltage too low.
Recommended Action: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Fault code 31 — typically maps to power module over-voltage. DC output or link voltage too high.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check power module regulation. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Fault code 34 — typically maps to power module over-current. Module exceeded current limit.
Recommended Action: Check for short circuit. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Fault code 51 — typically maps to contactor/relay stuck closed. Contactor can't open. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor. Check for welding.

What It Means: Fault code 52 — typically maps to contactor/relay stuck open. Contactor can't close to deliver power.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Replace contactor.

What It Means: Fault code 53 — typically maps to isolation fault. DC insulation resistance below minimum.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check cables. Megger test. Replace damaged cables.

What It Means: Fault code 54 — typically maps to precharge fault. Precharge circuit didn't complete properly.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check precharge components. Replace power module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: CPNK data partition is full. Station may stop storing session data, logs, or accepting firmware updates.
POA: Clear logs and old data. Reboot. Manual cleanup via shell. Escalate to Net Ops/ISE.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: CPNK temp partition is full. Firmware updates and temp file operations will fail.
POA: Reboot to clear temp files. If persists, manual cleanup via shell.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Speaker communication failed. Audio feedback (beeps, prompts) won't work. Station still charges normally.
POA: Check speaker cable. Replace speaker module. Non-critical.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Power module fault — all power modules shut down due to general fault status. May be caused by external source (vehicle).
POA: If occurring with other faults, escalate via #cpe250_os_support. Possible replacement needed.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Cable insulation check detected short circuit. DC cables or connector damaged. Safety hazard.
POA: Inspect cable for damage. Megger test. Replace cable if shorted.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Station detected invalid or unsupported cable. EEPROM data doesn't match.
POA: Verify correct cable installed. Check cable EEPROM. Replace cable.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Vehicle initiated emergency shutdown — vehicle ended charge unexpectedly. This is informational.
POA: Fault should clear at session end. If consistent across sessions, escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Power module failure — all PMs shut down. Often triggered by external source stopping session (vehicle).
POA: If occurring with other faults, escalate via #cpe250_os_support. Possible replacement.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Paired station has a critical fault. This station may be affected if it depends on shared power.
POA: Check paired station for faults. Fix paired station first.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Critical internal communication failure. Station controller can't talk to critical subsystems.
POA: Power cycle. Check all internal cables. Replace Falcon/DCC if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Multiple concurrent sessions detected on a single port. Software or hardware glitch.
POA: Power cycle. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Invalid vehicle communication sequence — vehicle didn't respond correctly to session start request.
POA: Vehicle-related fault. Should clear at end of session.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Vehicle did not end session properly — vehicle shutdown failure. Contactors may have opened under load.
POA: Should clear at session end. Try different vehicle. Check contactors for arc damage if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Vehicle requested a voltage below the station's minimum allowed value.
POA: Vehicle-related. Should clear at end of session.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Paired stations unable to communicate — DC wiring of paired stations is incorrect. Installation issue.
POA: DC wiring needs to be corrected per install guide. Fault clears once DC wiring is correct.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Unable to contact peer CPE250 / paired peer station not responding.
POA: Check both stations powered up. Match SW versions. Reboot both paired stations. Check ethernet wiring. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Session power management failed. Station couldn't properly manage power delivery.
POA: Power cycle. Check power modules. Escalate if recurring.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Paired station configuration does not match across both stations.
POA: Confirm stations meet pairing requirements. Make sure SW versions match (check OnRamp info). Reboot both stations. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Cable was unplugged during session. Informational — normal if driver removed cable.
POA: No action if intentional. Check connector latch if unexpected.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: GPIO failure on DCC. Hardware control pins not responding.
POA: Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Cable I/O failure. Station can't read or control cable signals (pilot, proximity, lock).
POA: Check cable controller wiring. Power cycle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: +12V internal supply is out of range.
POA: Check DCC power supply. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: -12V internal supply is out of range.
POA: Check DCC power supply. Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: CCS-A control pilot voltage out of range. Station can't communicate with vehicle via pilot signal.
POA: Check pilot circuit. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: CCS-B control pilot voltage out of range.
POA: Check pilot circuit. Test with different vehicle. Replace cable controller if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: CCS-A proximity pilot signal out of range. Station can't detect cable connection properly.
POA: Check proximity circuit and connector. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: CCS-B proximity pilot signal out of range.
POA: Check proximity circuit. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: DCC temperature sensor has completely failed. Station can't monitor DCC temperature.
POA: Replace DCC or temp sensor.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: DCC temperature sensor reading is erratic but still partially functional.
POA: Monitor. Replace DCC if readings become unreliable.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: EEPROM read error on DCC. Configuration or calibration data couldn't be read.
POA: Power cycle. Replace DCC if persists. EEPROM may be corrupted.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: PLC board 0 (port 1 CCS communication) is not responding. Vehicle communication impossible.
POA: Check PLC board seating. Power cycle. Replace PLC board if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: PLC board 1 (port 2 CCS communication) is not responding.
POA: Check PLC board seating. Power cycle. Replace PLC board if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 1 (main DC contactor) failed open check — may be welded shut.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 1. Check for arc damage.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 1 failed close check — stuck open or coil failed.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 1.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 1 auxiliary contact feedback mismatch.
POA: Check aux contact wiring. Replace contactor 1.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 2 failed open check — may be welded shut.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 2 failed close check — stuck open or coil failed.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 2 auxiliary contact feedback mismatch.
POA: Check aux wiring. Replace contactor 2.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 3 (precharge contactor) failed open check — may be welded.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 3 failed close check — stuck open or coil failed.
POA: Power cycle. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 3 auxiliary contact feedback mismatch.
POA: Check wiring. Replace contactor 3.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: All relays failed open check simultaneously. Multiple contactors welded or control circuit failed. Severe safety issue.
POA: Do not charge. Inspect all contactors. Replace contactors and DCC. Escalate.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Relay 3 not-closed check failed. On newer builds, this relay has been removed.
POA: Update software to 7.3.0.21+ to resolve. Known Issues

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC current sensor not responding. Station can't measure charging current. Safety system blind.
POA: Power cycle. Check current sensor wiring. Replace sensor or DCC if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Isolation Monitor CAN communication failed. Can't verify DC system isolation. Safety hazard.
POA: Power cycle. Check CAN cable to IsoMon. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: System voltage check failed during self-test. Internal voltage rails out of spec.
POA: Power cycle. Check DCC power supply. Replace DCC if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: RISO (insulation resistance) state check failed. Can't verify isolation. Safety hazard.
POA: Power cycle. Check IsoMon. Check DC cables for insulation damage. Replace IsoMon.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Isolation Monitor status not stable — readings fluctuating. Can't reliably confirm insulation integrity.
POA: Power cycle. Check IsoMon CAN cable. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Cooling Controller CAN communication failed. Can't manage cooling.
POA: Check CAN cable to CCB. Power cycle. Replace CCB if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Can't read Power Module Carrier Config. DCC unable to read EEPROM from carrier.
POA: Power cycle. Reseat PM carrier. Replace carrier or DCC if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Station I/O board CAN communication failed. Can't control station I/O (door, locks, LEDs).
POA: Check CAN cable to I/O board. Power cycle. Replace I/O board if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Smart Power CAN communication failed. Power management offline.
POA: Check CAN cable to SmartPwr. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Not all power module output channels turned on during startup. Some modules may be faulted.
POA: Power cycle. Check PM status. Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: General fan speed mismatch — actual RPM doesn't match commanded speed.
POA: Check fans for obstructions. Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: No power modules detected (variant). DCC can't find any PM on CAN bus.
POA: Check PM seating. Check CAN bus. Power cycle. Replace DCC if PMs are physically present.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Some power modules are not active. Station charges at reduced power.
POA: Check which modules are faulted (DCC logs). Replace faulted modules.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Voltage over 50V on PM output when station should be off/standalone. Safety hazard.
POA: Check if station should be paired. If yes, apply paired policy. If no, escalate to #cpe250_os_support. Fault Codes (CPE250)

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Unexpected voltage on both PMs' input bus. Voltage present where there shouldn't be any.
POA: Check AC input. Power cycle. Investigate wiring. Replace DCC/PMs if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Charge control CAN bus completely down. All CAN communication for charging has failed.
POA: Check CAN bus wiring and termination (120Ω). Power cycle. Replace DCC.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: No power modules detected. Station can't charge at all.
POA: Check PM connection (Mod-Mech press down). Verify 2 PMs installed. Check for connector damage. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: MCU firmware update failed on DCC or subsystem.
POA: Retry firmware update. Power cycle. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Voltage over 50V on PM output in standalone. Two root causes: PM failure or paired stations not set up correctly.
POA: A) Check if station should be paired — apply paired policy. B) If not paired, escalate to ISE via #cpe250_os_support. May need to disable faulted PM.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Unexpected voltage on both PMs' input bus. Often caused by incorrect transformer configuration (Delta secondary instead of Wye).
POA: Check transformer config — must be Delta-Wye (480V/277V). If Corner Grounded Delta, transformer needs rewiring. Fault Code 336 Case Study

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Entire power system has failed. Station cannot produce DC power.
POA: Power cycle. Check AC input. Check power modules. Replace DCC or PMs as needed.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: System self-test failed during startup. One or more subsystems didn't pass diagnostics.
POA: Power cycle a few times. Check DCC logs. Replace faulted component.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Isolation between safety ground and charger output is too low.
POA: Should clear at session end. If recurring, check SelfTest for PM "Failed" under Energize test. If one PM failed, disable it via #cpe250_os_support. Replace failed PM. Isolation Inspection Guide

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Emergency stop initiated by the station.
POA: Should clear with session. If persists, usually related to isolation faults. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: IsoMon invalid command / contactor relay issue.
