POA: Replace contactor box. DC Fuse Failure Guide

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Safety isolation monitor failed during self-test.
POA: Run another self-test. If every test fails, escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 1 (contactor) stuck closed — can't open. Safety hazard.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 2 stuck closed.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 3 stuck closed.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 1 stuck open — can't close to deliver power.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 2 stuck open.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Relay 3 stuck open.
POA: Replace contactor box.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Bus bar 1 is overheating. High-current connection is too hot. Fire hazard.
POA: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque. Inspect for discoloration. Re-torque or replace.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Bus bar 2 overheating.
POA: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Bus bar 3 overheating.
POA: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Tamper detected on Isolation Monitor. IsoMon enclosure opened or tamper switch triggered.
POA: Check IsoMon enclosure. Resecure.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Cable insulation resistance test failed. Safety hazard.
POA: Inspect cable for physical damage. Megger test cable. Replace cable if failed.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Proximity pilot (PP) signal error — cable not properly detected or proximity circuit fault.
POA: Check connector seating. Check PP circuit wiring. Replace cable if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Cable temperature near max threshold. Charging speed will be reduced (derated).
POA: Certain vehicles cause connector heating. Try different vehicle. If persists on multiple, escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Cable temperature reached alert level — charging stopped.
POA: Check cable for damage. Try different vehicle. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Left bank fan has been disabled. Fan not spinning at expected RPM. Station won't charge at full power.
POA: Check for fan obstructions. Replace fan with fault. Fan trays identified from front of station. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Right bank fan disabled. Same as above for right side.
POA: Check for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Coolant pump air lock — pump running but not circulating coolant. Air trapped in system.
POA: Update SW to 7.0.5.27+. Run self-test x2. Follow Airlock Clear Procedure. Tech needs to "burp" the system. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Fan tray is missing from module carrier slot. Fan not physically present.
POA: Reseat fan tray. Verify hardware installation.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Fan fault on power module carrier. Cooling for power modules compromised.
POA: Check PM carrier fans. Replace fan assembly.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Coolant level critically low. Dispatch immediately. Risk of overheating and pump damage.
POA: Dispatch immediately for possible leak or refill. Check for associated faults 10074, 10073, 10036, 601. Overheating Faults Guide

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Coolant at medium-low level. Known issue on multiple CPE250s. Not critical unless followed by 1007.
POA: No action needed unless followed by 1007 (which indicates actual leak).

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Airflow partially constricted. Station may derate under load.
POA: Check air filters and vents. Remove obstructions.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: PM internal fan showing signs of failure (speed mismatch).
POA: Check PM fan. Warning only. Schedule replacement.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Airflow severely constricted. Station will derate or overheat.
POA: Immediately clear obstructions. Check filters, vents, and fan operation.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Coolant pump has failed completely. No coolant circulation. Station will overheat. Lockout after 3 trips in 24h.
POA: Update SW to 7.0.5.27+. Run self-test x2. Follow Airlock Clear Procedure. Check pump and hoses. Replace coolant pump if persists. Escalate via #cpe250_os_support.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Three-phase gate driver faulted. Power module can't switch properly. Charging stops.
POA: Power cycle. Replace power module if persists. Escalate to engineering.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Front bottom panel door not installed correctly. Station UI changes to show panel status.
POA: All panels use magnetic connection. Verify panels make connection with magnet. See FRU guides for Front Panel and Rear Panel.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Middle vent panel door not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection. Check sensor in frame. Replace if defective.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Front top panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Secondary display panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Area light bar panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Rear bottom panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Rear middle panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Rear top panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Left extrusion panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Right extrusion panel not installed correctly.
POA: Verify magnet connection.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Flood sensor triggered — water detected inside station. Electrical hazard.
POA: Stop station. Inspect for water ingress. Check seals, cable glands, drainage. Dry out before restarting.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: External emergency stop triggered. E-stop button pressed.
POA: Check E-stop. Reset. Investigate why triggered.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC surge protector 1 tripped. AC surge protection depleted.
POA: Replace surge protector 1. Check site power quality.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC surge protector missing or not detected. Station has no surge protection.
POA: Install/replace surge protector.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: AC input temperature too hot. Loose connections or undersized wire. Fire hazard.
POA: Immediate attention. Check terminal torque. Inspect for discoloration/melting. Re-torque or replace.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DispIO lost CAN communication with station controller. I/O monitoring offline.
POA: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace DispIO if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC surge protector 2 tripped.
POA: Replace surge protector 2.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: SmartPwr lost CAN communication. Power distribution status unknown.
POA: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: SmartPwr CAN bus in non-operational state. Bus error recovery failed.
POA: Power cycle. Check CAN wiring and termination (120Ω). Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: SmartPwr fan 0 not spinning. No cooling for SmartPwr board.
POA: Check fan for obstructions. Replace fan.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: SmartPwr fan 1 not spinning.
POA: Check fan. Replace fan.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 0 output below minimum voltage. Subsystem may not function.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 1 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 2 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 3 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 4 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 0 output above max. Regulation failure.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 1 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 2 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 3 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 4 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 6 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 7 undervoltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 6 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 7 overvoltage.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 0 overcurrent. Subsystem drawing too much. Channel may shut down.
POA: Check subsystem for short. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 1 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 2 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 3 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 4 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 6 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 7 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC input to SmartPwr is over voltage. Site power too high.
POA: Check site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: SmartPwr in test mode during normal operation. Should not be in test mode.
POA: Exit test mode. Power cycle if stuck.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: PLL locked — SmartPwr synced to AC frequency. Usually informational.
POA: Monitor. Usually normal.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: General SmartPwr power fault. Internal power distribution failed.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 5 output below minimum voltage.
POA: Check load. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Channel 5 output above max.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Channel 5 overcurrent.
POA: Check subsystem for short. Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: SmartPwr internal hardware fault. Board-level failure.
POA: Power cycle. Replace SmartPwr.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: SmartPwr firmware error.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace SmartPwr if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: PM disabled — faulted 3 times in a row during charging sessions. PM won't participate until power cycled.
POA: Find associated alarms to confirm PM needs replacement (e.g., multiple 10072 faults before strikeout). Escalate via #cpe250_os_support. DO NOT RESET DCC!

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: AC input frequency out of range (not 50/60Hz ±limits). PM can't sync.
POA: Check input AC panel for correct supply. If on generator, verify frequency.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Ambient temp around PM above warning threshold. Self-clearing/informational.
POA: No troubleshooting or action needed. Check cooling system if persistent.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Rectifier (AC-DC stage) temperature above warning threshold. Self-clearing/informational.
POA: No troubleshooting or action needed. Check cooling system if persistent.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Charger (DC-DC) stage temp above warning. Self-clearing/informational.
POA: No action needed. Check cooling system if persistent.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC phases connected in wrong order. PM can't operate.
POA: Site electrician must swap two of the three phases at input.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: PM temp sensor failed. Module can't monitor own temperature.
POA: Replace power module.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Excessive humidity inside station. Risk of condensation on electronics.
POA: Check seals, cable glands, door gaskets. Inspect for water ingress.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Conditions favorable for condensation. Informational.
POA: Monitor humidity. Check seals. Ensure ventilation.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: DC link input to charger stage below minimum.
POA: Check AC input. Check rectifier stage. Power cycle.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Internal transformer temp above warning.
POA: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Watchdog triggered processor reset. Transient firmware glitch. Informational.
POA: Monitor. If frequent, replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: CPU1 executed illegal instruction. Firmware corruption or hardware fault.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: NMI triggered on CPU1. Critical hardware or firmware error.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: CPU1 reset unexpectedly. May be watchdog or transient.
POA: Monitor. Replace module if frequent.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Unhandled exception in CPU1 firmware. Software crash.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC bus discharge failed — voltage didn't bleed down after charging. Safety hazard — HV may persist.
POA: Do not open station. Wait and retry. Check discharge resistor. Replace if failed.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Multiple temp sensors disagree. One may be drifting.
POA: Monitor. Replace module if readings diverge significantly.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: Module actively derating due to temperature. Self-protection.
POA: Check cooling. Self-clears when temp drops.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC input voltage above maximum. Site voltage too high.
POA: Check site power. May need utility investigation.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC input voltage below minimum.
POA: Check site power. Check breakers and wiring.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: AC overcurrent — PM drawing more than rated.
POA: Check wiring. Check for short. Replace module if internal fault.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC output voltage exceeded maximum.
POA: Try different vehicle. Check regulation. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC output current exceeded maximum.
POA: Check for short in cable/contactor. Replace module if internal fault.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC link bus voltage too high.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: DC link bus voltage too low. Rectifier not providing enough.
POA: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace module if rectifier failed.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: General charger (DC-DC) stage fault. Can't convert to required output. Often seen before 10000 (strikeout).
POA: Power cycle. Replace module. Check if followed by 10000 strikeout — if yes, module needs replacement.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Rectifier exceeded critical temp — shut down. Station charges at reduced power.
POA: This is an overheating fault. Follow CPE250 Overheating Faults - 10074, 1007, 601. Check cooling. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Charger stage exceeded critical temp — shut down.
POA: Follow CPE250 Overheating Faults - 10074, 1007, 601. Check cooling. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Internal communication error within module (CPU-to-CPU or CPU-to-FPGA).
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Output relay inside module has failed. Can't connect/disconnect from DC bus.
POA: Replace power module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Charger didn't reach target voltage during startup.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: DC output below target. Vehicle may not accept charge.
POA: Check module. Try different vehicle. Replace if regulation failed.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Output channel A not ready. Failed startup check.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Output channel B not ready.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Internal switching frequency below expected. Control loop issue.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Voltage range switch failed. Can't change voltage range.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: DC link undervoltage caused shutdown.
POA: Check AC input. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Ambient temp exceeded max — module shut down.
POA: Environmental. Wait for temp to drop. Shade structure may be needed.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Internal transformer exceeded max temp — shut down.
POA: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if transformer damaged.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: LLC converter primary overcurrent.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: AC input current imbalance between phases.
POA: Check AC wiring. Check for failed rectifier leg. Replace module if internal.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Voltage target sent to module is outside supported range.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Escalate if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Module timed out during startup. Didn't reach ready state in time.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: EPO activated. E-stop pressed or EPO circuit triggered.
POA: Check E-stop. Reset EPO. Investigate trigger cause.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: DC link voltage didn't reach setpoint. Precharge or rectifier underperforming.
POA: Power cycle. Check precharge circuit. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Precharge circuit fault. Can't safely bring up DC link.
POA: Power cycle. Check precharge components. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Module EEPROM error. Config/calibration data corrupted.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if permanent.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Internal 24V rail failed. Control circuits have no power.
POA: Replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Internal 5V rail failed. Digital logic has no power.
POA: Replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Internal 3.3V rail failed. Processor has no power.
POA: Replace module.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Module lost CAN communication with station controller.
POA: Check CAN cabling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Module didn't reach ready state within expected time.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: Current sensor failed. Module can't measure output current. Safety hazard.
POA: Replace module.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: PFC stage temp above warning.
POA: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: Minor
Recommended Action: LLC converter stage temp above warning.
POA: Monitor. Check cooling.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: IPC between CPU1 and CPU2 failed. Dual-CPU module can't coordinate.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: IPC failure from CPU2 side.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: CPU2 executed illegal instruction. Firmware corruption.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: NMI on CPU2. Critical HW or FW error.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Major
Recommended Action: Unhandled exception on CPU2. Software crash.
POA: Power cycle. Update firmware. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: CPU2 failed to boot. Secondary processor didn't start.
POA: Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: PFC stage exceeded critical temp — shut down.
POA: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: LLC converter exceeded critical temp — shut down.
POA: Check cooling. Power cycle. Replace module if persists.

What It Means: Critical
Recommended Action: CPU2 boot failure — secondary processor permanently failed.
POA: Replace module.

What It Means: USCI enclosure door is open. Tamper switch triggered.
Recommended Action: Close and secure door. Check latch.

What It Means: AC surge protector in USCI has tripped and needs replacement.
Recommended Action: Replace AC surge protector.

What It Means: 48V surge protector in USCI has tripped.
Recommended Action: Replace 48V surge protector.

What It Means: USCI I/O module is unreachable. Can't control pantograph or monitor sensors.
Recommended Action: Check I/O module connections. Power cycle. Replace I/O module if persists.

What It Means: Wireless access point for EV communication is unreachable. Can't establish wireless link with vehicle.
Recommended Action: Check wireless AP connections. Power cycle AP. Replace AP if persists.

What It Means: RFID reader in USCI is unreachable. Can't authenticate vehicles via RFID.
Recommended Action: Check RFID reader connections. Power cycle. Replace reader if persists.

What It Means: Pantograph retract timed out — pantograph arm didn't return to rest position in time.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstructions. Check motor. Power cycle.

What It Means: Pantograph extend timed out — arm didn't reach vehicle contact in time.
Recommended Action: Check for obstructions. Verify vehicle positioning. Check motor.

What It Means: Pantograph extend timeout — critical level. Multiple failed attempts. Station offline.
Recommended Action: Check motor, mechanism, and positioning. Replace motor if failed.

What It Means: Pantograph rest position sensor error — can't detect if arm is in home position.
Recommended Action: Check rest sensor. Replace sensor if persists.

What It Means: Pantograph was extended but position signal lost. Arm position unknown. Safety concern.
Recommended Action: Check position sensors. Manually retract if possible. Replace sensors.

What It Means: Pantograph was retracted but position signal lost.
Recommended Action: Check position sensors. Replace if persists.

What It Means: Pantograph position is completely unknown. All position sensors returning invalid data.
Recommended Action: Do not operate. Check all sensors. Replace sensor assembly.

What It Means: Pantograph stuck at rest — motor running but arm not moving. Mechanism may be jammed.
Recommended Action: Check for physical obstructions. Check motor. Replace motor or mechanism if jammed.

What It Means: Pantograph position signal error — sensor readings inconsistent.
Recommended Action: Check position sensor wiring. Replace sensor if persists.

What It Means: EV abnormally closed communication. Vehicle terminated wireless link unexpectedly.
Recommended Action: Try again. Check wireless AP. May be vehicle-side issue.

What It Means: Wireless communication with EV lost during session.
Recommended Action: Check wireless AP. Check vehicle positioning. Retry session.

What It Means: Pantograph can't align with vehicle contact pads. Vehicle may be mispositioned.
Recommended Action: Reposition vehicle. Check pantograph alignment sensors.

What It Means: Pantograph didn't reach State C (charging active) in time. Vehicle or station failed to transition to charging state.
Recommended Action: Check vehicle readiness. Check pilot signal. Retry session.

What It Means: Isolation monitoring timed out during pantograph charging. Can't verify DC isolation. Safety hazard.
Recommended Action: Power cycle. Check IsoMon. Replace IsoMon if persists.

What It Means: Communication performance time violated — response latency exceeded threshold. May cause session quality issues.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check network. Usually transient.

What It Means: RFID PPD (Pantograph Positioning Device) tag not found on vehicle. Can't verify vehicle identity/position.
Recommended Action: Check if vehicle has PPD tag installed. Check RFID reader range.

What It Means: RFID PPD tag found but it's the wrong tag — vehicle identity mismatch.
Recommended Action: Verify correct vehicle. Check tag configuration.

What It Means: SEVB (Smart EV Board) for USC is unreachable. Pantograph charging controller offline.
Recommended Action: Check SEVB connections. Power cycle. Replace SEVB if persists.

What It Means: Manual power override is active — operator manually controlling power delivery. Informational.
Recommended Action: No action unless unexpected. Check if authorized operator is present.

What It Means: ZLB adapter controller lost CAN communication with station. Eichrecht metering and adapter management offline.
Recommended Action: Check CAN cabling to ZLB. Power cycle. Replace ZLB controller if persists.

What It Means: Left ZLB adapter not detected. Adapter may have been removed or connection lost.
Recommended Action: Check left adapter seating. Verify blindmate connection. Replace adapter if persists.

What It Means: Right ZLB adapter not detected.
Recommended Action: Check right adapter seating. Replace adapter if persists.

What It Means: Left adapter lock motor has failed. Adapter can't be locked in position.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace lock motor or ZLB assembly.

What It Means: Left holster lock motor has failed. Cable connector can't be locked in holster.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace lock motor.

What It Means: Right adapter lock motor failed.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace lock motor or ZLB assembly.

What It Means: Right holster lock motor failed.
Recommended Action: Check motor wiring. Replace lock motor.

What It Means: Left connector is having difficulty releasing from adapter. Lock mechanism may be sticking.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check lock mechanism. Lubricate if needed.

What It Means: Left connector is stuck in adapter — can't be removed. Lock motor or mechanism has failed.
Recommended Action: Check lock motor. Manually release if possible. Replace ZLB assembly if persists.

What It Means: Right connector difficulty releasing from adapter.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check lock mechanism.

What It Means: Right connector stuck in adapter.
Recommended Action: Check lock motor. Manually release. Replace ZLB assembly.

What It Means: Left adapter having difficulty releasing from holster.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check holster mechanism.

What It Means: Left adapter stuck in holster — can't be removed.
Recommended Action: Check holster lock motor. Manually release. Replace holster mechanism.

What It Means: Right adapter difficulty releasing from holster.
Recommended Action: Monitor. Check holster mechanism.

What It Means: Right adapter stuck in holster.
Recommended Action: Check holster lock motor. Replace holster mechanism.

What It Means: 12V supply to ZLB controller is below minimum. Lock motors and sensors may not function properly.
Recommended Action: Check 12V supply from AuxPS. Check wiring. Replace ZLB controller if voltage is OK at source but low at ZLB.

What It Means: 5V supply to ZLB controller is below minimum. Logic circuits and sensors may fail.
Recommended Action: Check 5V regulator on ZLB. Replace ZLB controller if persists.
